workspace.name: ANIP Showcase Packages
workspace.summary: Canonical public showcase projects used to publish ANIP registry packages and restore read-only Studio snapshots.

project.name: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3
project.summary: Canonical Jira fronting showcase package for governed Jira reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated changes, audit evidence, and safe denial of raw tool access.

business_constraints.assistant_drafted_requirements_summary:
Expose a governed Jira capability surface instead of raw Jira operations: The product shall present business-facing Jira capabilities for bounded context lookup, issue and comment preparation, governed change requests, and release-note preparation. It shall not expose raw Jira REST, MCP operations, raw Jira tools, or direct raw JQL access to agents or end users.
Return bounded, actor-visible Jira context for read flows: The product shall support bounded Jira read capabilities for backlog context search and single-issue context retrieval. Read results must be limited to actor-visible fields and bounded context, including optional comments and history where allowed, and must avoid unbounded issue export.
Require clarification for missing, ambiguous, or unresolved required inputs: For governed Jira capabilities, the product shall require clarification when required inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved through backend catalogs. It shall not guess project keys, issue references, statuses, assignees, sprint targets, releases, or link types.
Support preview-only preparation for issue and comment creation flows: The product shall support preview preparation flows for incident bugs, stories, subtasks, and customer escalation comments. These flows must produce drafts or previews for review without directly creating Jira issues or posting comments as part of the initial request.
Gate Jira-changing requests behind explicit approval boundaries: The product shall support governed request flows for workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links. These flows must stop at an approval boundary when policy requires it, returning a preview of the intended change and the stated business reason before any execution occurs.
Deny unsafe or out-of-policy Jira access explicitly: The product shall explicitly deny requests for raw Jira tool access, raw JQL exposure, unrestricted export, or other out-of-policy operations. Denial responses should be intentional and explain that only governed capabilities are available.
Produce audit evidence for governed reads, previews, approvals, and denials: The product shall preserve audit evidence for governed Jira interactions, including what capability was invoked, what bounded scope or target was requested, whether the result was a read, clarification, preview, approval stop, or denial, and enough context for later reviewer inspection.
Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issue context without publishing: The product shall support release-note preparation from bounded Jira issue and release context. It must generate draft release notes for supported audiences without publishing externally and without mutating Jira.
Resolve business references through governed Jira catalogs: The product shall resolve business references such as projects, issues, workflow statuses, sprints, assignees, releases, components, and issue link types through governed backend resolution rather than freeform execution. When resolution fails or is ambiguous, the system must clarify or deny according to policy.
Apply role-aware governance across bounded reads, change requests, and audit review: The product shall support distinct actor postures for triage, delivery coordination, release preparation, change approval, and audit review. Permissions must distinguish bounded access, approval-required actions, direct audit review access where intended, and denied governance-control access for non-review actors.

scenarios.title: Inspect one Jira issue with bounded comments and history

scenario.name: inspect-one-jira-issue-with-bounded-comments-and-history

context.rationale: Directly supported by the issue context capability, including bounded fields, comments, changelog options, and clarification on missing issue reference.
context.actor_context: jira_triage_operator inspecting a known Jira issue

scenario.narrative: A Jira triage operator needs context on a single issue before deciding next action. They request issue details, and the system returns bounded actor-visible fields with comments and, when requested, history.
expected_behavior: Return bounded issue details rather than raw Jira payloads.
Include comments by default.
Include changelog only when requested or otherwise configured.
Clarify missing or ambiguous issue references.
expected_anip_support: Expose a governed issue context capability.
Resolve issue references through governed catalogs rather than free-form backend access.
Keep the result actor-visible and bounded.
Record audit evidence for the read path.

scenarios.title: Search bounded Jira backlog context without exposing raw JQL

scenario.name: search-bounded-jira-backlog-context-without-exposing-raw-jql

context.rationale: Directly supported by the brief and capability evidence for bounded backlog search, clarification on missing inputs, and denial of raw Jira/JQL access.
context.time_scope: ad hoc triage and recurring backlog review
context.actor_context: jira_triage_operator reviewing bounded Jira context in an allowed project

scenario.narrative: A Jira triage operator needs to find relevant work in an allowed project. They provide a project and search intent, and the system returns bounded backlog context rather than raw Jira query access or unbounded exports.
expected_behavior: Return bounded issue context for an allowed project.
Require clarification when project scope or query is missing or ambiguous.
Do not expose raw JQL or raw Jira tool access.
Do not return unbounded issue export behavior.
expected_anip_support: Expose a governed read capability for backlog context search.
Enforce bounded result shaping and actor-visible scope.
Produce explicit denial outcomes for raw Jira or raw JQL requests.
Capture audit evidence for the governed read request.

scenarios.title: Prepare a Jira issue preview before any issue is created

scenario.name: prepare-a-jira-issue-preview-before-any-issue-is-created

context.rationale: Strongly supported by the prepare-only capabilities for incident bug, story, and subtask, all of which produce previews and stop before mutation.
context.actor_context: jira_triage_operator or jira_delivery_coordinator preparing Jira work items

scenario.narrative: An operator wants help drafting Jira work from incident, delivery, or parent-issue context. The system prepares a preview for a bug, story, or subtask and stops before any issue is created.
expected_behavior: Support preview-only preparation for bug, story, and subtask flows.
Require clarification for missing required inputs instead of guessing.
Return a draft or preview suitable for review.
Do not create the issue as part of the preparation scenario.
expected_anip_support: Expose governed prepare-only capabilities for issue drafting.
Represent preview outputs as explicit write-adjacent results.
Apply one-time or session-bound grant posture where relevant.
Capture audit evidence that the flow stopped before mutation.

scenarios.title: Prepare a customer escalation comment without posting it

scenario.name: prepare-a-customer-escalation-comment-without-posting-it

context.rationale: Directly supported by the customer escalation comment preparation capability, including purpose, visibility, and preview-only behavior.
context.actor_context: jira_triage_operator preparing incident or escalation communication

scenario.narrative: A Jira triage operator needs to communicate status or impact on an issue but must avoid posting directly. The system prepares a customer-safe comment preview with declared purpose and visibility, then stops before posting.
expected_behavior: Prepare a comment preview rather than posting directly.
Require a declared comment purpose and context.
Default visibility to internal unless another allowed value is explicitly chosen.
Clarify missing issue reference or purpose.
expected_anip_support: Expose a governed comment preparation capability.
Enforce preview-only behavior for write-adjacent comment flows.
Support visibility and purpose controls in the runtime contract.
Record audit evidence for the prepared comment request.

scenarios.title: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing

scenario.name: prepare-release-notes-from-bounded-jira-issues-without-publishing

context.rationale: Directly supported by the release notes preparation capability and the brief's emphasis on preview-only, non-mutating preparation.

scenario.narrative: A release preparer needs a draft summary of work for a Jira release. The system gathers bounded issue context for the specified release and query, then returns release notes tailored to the requested audience without publishing anything.
expected_behavior: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issue context.
Support audience shaping such as internal, customer, or executive when explicitly requested.
Clarify missing project, release, or query inputs.
Do not publish release notes or mutate Jira.
expected_anip_support: Expose a governed release note preparation capability.
Keep the output as draft content rather than external dispatch.
Enforce bounded issue retrieval and aggregation.
Capture audit evidence for the release note preparation request.

scenarios.title: Prepare a governed Jira change request and stop for approval

scenario.name: prepare-a-governed-jira-change-request-and-stop-for-approval

context.rationale: Directly supported by the approval-required request capabilities and the project summary emphasizing approval-gated changes.
context.time_scope: during delivery coordination and change control
context.actor_context: jira_delivery_coordinator preparing governed Jira changes; jira_change_approver reviews approval-required requests

scenario.narrative: A delivery coordinator needs to change Jira state in a governed way. The system prepares a workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, or issue link request, shows the preview, and stops for approval before execution.
expected_behavior: Support governed previews for workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link requests.
Require a business reason for the requested change.
Stop for approval before any Jira mutation occurs.
Clarify unresolved or ambiguous references instead of guessing.
expected_anip_support: Expose approval-required change request capabilities separate from raw execution.
Use governed resolution for statuses, sprints, assignees, and link types.
Represent approval stop as an explicit runtime outcome.
Capture audit evidence for the requested change and approval boundary.

scenarios.title: Deny raw Jira tool access and redirect to governed capabilities

scenario.name: deny-raw-jira-tool-access-and-redirect-to-governed-capabilities

context.rationale: Explicitly supported by the fronting intent, project summary, and user-provided brief calling for safe denial of raw tool access.
context.capability: No direct raw capability is exposed; denial is part of the governed fronting behavior
context.time_scope: whenever a raw tool access request is made
context.actor_context: any actor interacting with the Jira governed fronting surface

scenario.narrative: A user attempts to bypass the governed Jira surface by asking for raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or raw JQL. The system denies the request, explains the boundary, and redirects the user to supported governed capabilities.
expected_behavior: Do not expose raw Jira REST or MCP operations to the agent.
Do not allow raw JQL as a direct tool surface.
Return an explicit denial rather than silently failing or partially complying.
Redirect the user toward supported governed capabilities where possible.
expected_anip_support: Represent denial outcomes explicitly in the runtime contract.
Separate governed capabilities from hidden backend operations.
Capture audit evidence for denied raw access attempts.
Preserve policy boundaries consistently across actors.

scenarios.title: Review audit evidence for governed Jira reads, previews, denials, and approval stops

scenario.name: review-audit-evidence-for-governed-jira-outcomes

context.rationale: Audit evidence is explicit in the project summary and brief, but the exact evidence fields are not fully specified, so the scenario is concrete but still needs confirmation.
context.capability: Audit review behavior is supported by the governed fronting surface, but the exact audit retrieval capability is not named in the brief
context.actor_context: jira_audit_reviewer reviewing governance and audit controls

scenario.narrative: An audit reviewer needs to verify that Jira access and change preparation followed the governed surface. They inspect evidence showing what capability was requested, what boundary applied, and whether the result was a bounded read, preview, denial, clarification, or approval stop.
expected_behavior: Provide reviewable evidence for governed Jira interactions.
Show why a request was allowed, clarified, denied, or stopped for approval.
Support audit review without exposing raw backend secrets or tokens.
Preserve traceability across reads, previews, and approval-gated requests.
expected_anip_support: Capture audit evidence as part of governed runtime behavior.
Make outcome type and policy boundary visible for review.
Keep secret posture intact by storing references rather than tokens.
Support PM-visible auditability even if the exact audit retrieval interface is finalized later.

bundle.summary: Review this source-derived Product Design bundle, answer only blocking clarifications, and save accepted sections into deterministic Studio artifacts.

envelope.title: Business Summary Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Showcase
envelope.summary: Draft PM-facing product summary patches derived from the Jira brief. The source supports a lockable summary because purpose, problem, goals, governed behavior, approval posture, and stable question families are all evidenced in the brief and capability evidence.

patches.value: Provide a governed Jira fronting experience that lets users retrieve bounded Jira context and prepare reviewable Jira changes without exposing raw Jira tools or bypassing approval controls.
patches.rationale: The brief explicitly describes governed Jira reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated changes, audit evidence, and denial of raw tool access.

patches.value: Teams need Jira access and action support, but direct exposure to raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or raw JQL can create unsafe scope, inconsistent review, weak auditability, and uncontrolled mutations.
patches.rationale: The source repeatedly frames the problem as needing safe Jira access without exposing raw tools, unbounded search, or unreviewed writes.

value: Enable bounded, actor-visible Jira context retrieval for backlog and issue review.
Let users prepare Jira issue creation, comments, release notes, and change requests as previews before execution.
Require explicit approval for governed Jira changes such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links.
Deny direct raw Jira, raw JQL, and raw tool access in favor of governed business capabilities.
Preserve audit evidence for governed reads, previews, requests, and approvals.
patches.rationale: These goals are directly supported by the project summary, fronting intent, and capability evidence.
The capability set provides stable user task families the product is meant to answer through governed Jira fronting.

patches.value: The product exposes governed Jira capabilities instead of raw Jira operations. Reads return bounded actor-visible context, write-adjacent actions produce previews or requests rather than direct execution, missing required inputs trigger clarification, and unsafe raw access requests are explicitly denied.
patches.rationale: This is stated in the governance posture and reinforced by the capability formalizations.

patches.value: Read-only context retrieval can proceed within bounded scope, while write-adjacent actions either stop at preview or require explicit approval before any Jira mutation is executed. Higher-impact changes are approval-gated and auditable.
patches.rationale: The evidence distinguishes read-only operations from prepare-only and approval-required actions.
The source provides enough concrete product intent and governance posture to support locking after user acceptance.

watchouts: Some source files are starter-template based, so lock only if this showcase is intended to preserve the documented governed Jira surface as-is.
The brief supports approval-gated change requests and preview-only preparation, but not autonomous execution of Jira mutations.
The product summary should stay at business-behavior level and avoid copying backend endpoint detail into PM-facing fields.
next_steps: Review whether the supported question families match the intended showcase scope for launch.
Confirm that denial of raw Jira, raw JQL, and raw tool access is a product-level promise, not just an implementation detail.
If accepted, promote these patches into the Product Summary and lock the artifact.

envelope.title: Actor Model Patch Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Showcase
envelope.summary: Draft actor additions for the Jira governed fronting showcase based on the brief’s governed read, preview, approval, denial, and audit intent. The source defines capability behavior clearly, but it does not explicitly name human actor ids, so these are candidate business-role actors rather than locked identities.

value.notes: Derived from the brief’s emphasis on bounded reads, issue preparation, customer-safe comment preparation, explicit denial of raw tool access, and clarification instead of guessing.
value.title: Jira Triage Operator
value.summary: Operational user who inspects bounded Jira issue and backlog context and prepares issue or comment drafts without direct raw Jira tool access.
value.action_expectations: Can search backlog context, inspect issue context, prepare incident bugs, prepare subtasks, and prepare customer escalation comments within governed input rules and clarification flows.
value.approval_expectations: Can initiate preview-oriented and approval-gated requests but should stop at explicit approval boundaries for governed mutations.
value.visibility_expectations: Can see bounded Jira project, issue, comment, changelog, and backlog context exposed through governed capabilities, plus previews and audit-relevant request context. Should not see raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL/tool access.

patches.rationale: The brief clearly implies an operational actor who consumes governed Jira context and prepares work without direct backend exposure.

value.notes: This actor groups the brief’s governed change-request capabilities that are operational but not final-execution authority.
value.title: Jira Delivery Coordinator
value.summary: Planning-oriented user who prepares and requests governed changes to issue workflow, sprint placement, assignee, and issue relationships.
value.action_expectations: Can request workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links through governed preview and request flows with stated business reasons.
value.approval_expectations: Can assemble and submit approval-required change requests, but execution authority is separate unless you confirm this actor is also an approver.
value.visibility_expectations: Can see actor-visible workflow, sprint, assignee-resolution, and issue-link preview context needed to understand proposed changes, but not raw backend operations or unrestricted Jira administration surfaces.

patches.rationale: The source defines a coherent family of approval-gated Jira change requests that suggests a delivery/planning actor distinct from read-only inspection.

value.notes: Kept separate because release-note preparation is a distinct business workflow in the source, even though it is read-only.
value.title: Jira Release Preparer
value.summary: Release-focused user who prepares bounded release notes from Jira issue context without publishing or mutating Jira.
value.action_expectations: Can prepare release notes drafts from governed Jira release and issue queries and tailor drafts for internal, customer, or executive audiences where allowed.
value.approval_expectations: Can prepare release-note drafts directly; approval or publication authority is not specified in the brief and should be clarified if needed.
value.visibility_expectations: Can see bounded release and issue context sufficient to draft release notes for an intended audience, without raw export or unrestricted issue extraction.

patches.rationale: Release-note preparation is explicitly called out as a business capability and may warrant its own actor if release communications are owned separately.

value.notes: Use only if your operating model includes a human approval step rather than a purely policy-driven gate.
value.title: Jira Change Approver
value.summary: Reviewing actor who evaluates governed Jira change requests before any approval-required mutation proceeds.
value.action_expectations: Can review workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests and decide whether they should proceed under governance.
value.approval_expectations: Acts as the explicit approval boundary for approval-required Jira changes, but exact authority by action type is not specified in the source and should be confirmed.
value.visibility_expectations: Can inspect the proposed change, target issue or sprint context, stated reason, and supporting audit evidence needed to approve or deny governed Jira mutations.

patches.rationale: The brief repeatedly emphasizes approval-gated changes, so an approver actor is a strong candidate even though the exact organizational owner is not named.

value.notes: Included because the brief explicitly requires audit evidence and safe denial behavior.
value.title: Jira Audit Reviewer
value.summary: Oversight actor who reviews audit evidence for governed Jira access, prepared changes, approvals, and denials.
value.action_expectations: Can review evidence of bounded reads, prepared write-adjacent actions, approval stops, and explicit denials of raw Jira/JQL/tool access.
value.approval_expectations: Typically not the operational approver unless your process combines audit and approval responsibilities; this should be clarified.
value.visibility_expectations: Can inspect audit evidence showing what governed capability was used, what was requested or denied, and where approval boundaries were enforced. Should not gain raw Jira tool access through the audit role.

patches.rationale: Audit evidence is a first-class requirement in the brief, which suggests an oversight actor distinct from day-to-day operators.

watchouts: The brief strongly describes governed capabilities but does not explicitly name actor ids, so proposed actor ids are inferred and should be validated before persistence.
Approval-required is an action posture, not an actor; do not encode approval states or policy terms as actor ids.
Internal adapter capabilities are implementation-facing and should not be modeled as end-user actors.
The brief says agents must not see raw Jira REST, MCP, or JQL/tool access; actor visibility should stay at governed capability level only.
Approval authority may differ across workflow, sprint, assignee, and link actions; avoid assuming one approver if the organization splits ownership.
Audit evidence is required by the brief, but the source does not specify whether auditors can only inspect evidence or also review denials and approvals.
next_steps: Confirm the real business actors that use this governed Jira surface.
Decide whether to keep a compact actor model or split by operational domain: triage, delivery, release, approval, audit.
Assign approval authority per governed write-adjacent request type.
Validate whether customer-facing communication needs its own actor.
After confirmation, accept the relevant patches and refine any actor summaries or approval expectations.
questions_for_user: Which business users should be modeled separately for this Jira fronting surface: issue triagers, delivery leads, release managers, approvers, or auditors?
Who is allowed to request approval-gated actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links?
Who holds approval authority for each approval-required action type, or is there one common approver across all governed Jira changes?
Should customer-visible comment preparation be available to the same actor as internal comment preparation, or to a narrower communications/support role?
Should release note preparation be modeled as a distinct release-focused actor, or as part of a broader delivery/release manager role?
Do you want a distinct audit/compliance actor for reviewing evidence and denials, or is that visibility folded into an approver role?

envelope.title: Business Areas Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Showcase
envelope.summary: Draft PM-facing business area patch candidates derived from the Jira governed fronting brief. These group the durable product responsibilities around bounded Jira access, preview-first change preparation, approval-gated requests, release drafting, and auditability.

value.label: Bounded Jira Context
value.description: Governed access to Jira backlog and issue context with bounded, actor-visible results instead of raw Jira or unbounded export.

patches.rationale: The brief centers on bounded Jira reads, safe context retrieval, and denial of raw Jira/JQL/tool exposure as a primary business responsibility.

value.label: Issue and Comment Preparation
value.description: Preview-first preparation of Jira bugs, stories, subtasks, and customer-safe comments before any change is executed.

patches.rationale: The source emphasizes issue preparation and write-adjacent preview flows that stop short of direct mutation.

value.label: Governed Change Requests
value.description: Approval-gated requests for workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links under explicit governance.

patches.rationale: Workflow and sprint-change previews, assignee-change requests, and issue-link requests are grouped by the shared approval-required change-control posture.

value.label: Release Note Preparation
value.description: Preparation of bounded release-note drafts from Jira issue and release context without publishing or mutating Jira.

patches.rationale: Release-note preparation is called out explicitly in the brief and represents a distinct PM-facing outcome.

value.label: Governance and Audit Controls
value.description: Product controls that enforce approval gates, explicit denial of raw Jira access, and audit evidence for governed operations.

patches.rationale: The brief explicitly requires approval-gated changes, safe denial of raw tool access, and audit evidence, which together form a durable governance area.

watchouts: The source is a showcase/template, so business areas should reflect durable product responsibilities, not starter-template wording.
Avoid creating business areas for internal adapter mechanics; PM-facing areas should describe user-visible value and governance intent.
Several capabilities are preview-only or approval-gated; do not label them as direct execution or unrestricted Jira automation.
Raw Jira REST, MCP, and JQL denial is a core product boundary and should remain explicit in PM vocabulary.
Too many narrow areas will make later capability mapping noisy; too few will hide important governance distinctions.
next_steps: Review whether these areas match the product story you want PMs and reviewers to use.
Decide if release-note preparation should remain standalone or merge into bounded Jira context/reporting.
Confirm preferred stability and naming style for business_area_id values before acceptance.
After acceptance, map capabilities to the approved business areas and check for any missing governance coverage.
questions_for_user: Do you want separate business areas for read-only Jira context vs write-adjacent change preparation, or should they stay grouped under one Jira operations area?
Should audit evidence and raw-tool denial live in one governance area, or be split into governance and compliance/audit areas?
Is release-note preparation important enough to stand as its own business area, or should it remain inside a broader Jira planning/reporting area?
Do you want business area ids to stay Jira-specific for this showcase, or be generalized for reuse across other governed fronting packages?

envelope.title: Permission Intent Patch Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Showcase
envelope.summary: Draft permission-intent patches align Jira access to bounded reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated change requests, explicit denial of raw Jira/JQL/tool access, and audit-oriented review. Actor and business-area ids are supported by the in-flight design context embedded in the brief.

patches.value: Jira access is governed through bounded business-facing capabilities only. Reads return bounded actor-visible context, write-adjacent actions return previews or requests, approval-gated changes stop for explicit approval, missing required inputs trigger clarification, raw Jira/JQL/tool access is denied, and audit evidence remains reviewable through governed surfaces.
patches.rationale: Summarizes the explicit governance posture stated across the brief and fronting intent.

value.notes: Matches bounded context search and issue context inspection in the brief.
value.governed_outcome: Allow bounded Jira backlog and issue context needed for triage without exposing raw JQL, raw tool access, or unbounded export.

patches.rationale: Triage operators need governed read access to bounded Jira context.

value.notes: Preparation remains preview-only and non-mutating from the actor perspective.
value.governed_outcome: Allow preparation previews for incident bugs, subtasks, and customer escalation comments without creating issues or posting comments directly.

patches.rationale: The brief explicitly includes issue preparation and customer-safe comment preparation as governed capabilities.

value.governed_outcome: Allow workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests to be prepared with preview and audit context, then stop for explicit approval before execution.

patches.rationale: The brief calls for workflow and sprint-change previews, assignee-change requests, issue-link requests, and approval-required write-adjacent actions.

value.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of release-note drafts from bounded Jira issue and release context without publishing or mutating Jira.

patches.rationale: The brief explicitly includes release-note preparation as a governed capability.

value.notes: Frames the approver as the approval checkpoint, not a raw tool operator.
value.governed_outcome: Receive governed change-request packages with preview and rationale, and act as the explicit approval boundary before any Jira mutation proceeds.

patches.rationale: The actor list includes a change approver and the brief requires explicit approval before execution.

value.notes: Audit review is evidence-focused rather than operational control.
value.governed_outcome: Allow access to governed audit evidence and review artifacts for Jira fronting decisions, approvals, and denials.

patches.rationale: The brief explicitly includes audit evidence and an audit reviewer actor exists in the accepted design context.

value.notes: Applies the fronting boundary to business-facing actors.
value.governed_outcome: Deny requests for raw Jira REST access, MCP access, raw JQL execution, or direct tool use outside the governed capability surface.

patches.rationale: The brief explicitly requires denial of raw Jira/JQL/tool access and says agents must see governed capabilities, not raw operations.

value.notes: Prevents coordinators from bypassing approval-gated flows.
value.governed_outcome: Deny direct raw Jira, MCP, and raw JQL access even when coordinating change requests; use governed request surfaces only.

patches.rationale: Delivery coordination should still remain inside the governed fronting model.

value.notes: Keeps release-note drafting within the fronted read-only surface.
value.governed_outcome: Deny raw Jira/JQL/tool access for release-note work; release-note preparation must use bounded governed capabilities only.

patches.rationale: The brief requires safe denial of raw tool access across the governed Jira showcase.

value.notes: Clarification is part of the governance posture for bounded reads.
value.governed_outcome: When required Jira identifiers or scope inputs are missing or ambiguous, stop and ask for clarification instead of guessing project, issue, status, or query scope.

patches.rationale: The source repeatedly states that missing required inputs should trigger clarification instead of guessing.

watchouts: The source supports actor ids from the in-flight design context, but it does not fully map every capability to every actor; some actor-to-area coverage remains policy choice.
Approval-required change flows are preview/request surfaces, not direct execution rights; avoid wording that implies mutation authority before approval.
Raw Jira REST, MCP operations, and raw JQL exposure should be denied explicitly so downstream policy does not accidentally treat adapter capabilities as user-facing.
Release-note preparation is read-only draft generation, but audience and issue-query inputs can still widen exposure if not bounded in later policy detail.
next_steps: Review whether the proposed rules reflect the intended separation between operators, coordinators, release preparers, approvers, and audit reviewers.
Confirm any additional actor-specific restrictions for release-note preparation and audit visibility.
If accepted, persist these patches and then refine capability-level bindings in downstream implementation policy.
questions_for_user: Should jira_change_approver be allowed to receive approval-stop packages only, or also view the same bounded Jira context available to operators?
Should jira_audit_reviewer have read access only to governance and audit evidence, or also to bounded issue/change-request context for spot checks?
Do you want any actor-specific denials beyond raw Jira/JQL/tool access, such as restricting release-note preparation to jira_release_preparer only?

envelope.title: Requirement Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Showcase
envelope.summary: Candidate PM requirement blocks for a governed Jira fronting package that exposes bounded Jira reads, preview-only preparation flows, approval-gated change requests, explicit denial of raw Jira access, and audit evidence.

items.body: The product shall present business-facing Jira capabilities for bounded context lookup, issue and comment preparation, governed change requests, and release-note preparation. It shall not expose raw Jira REST, MCP operations, raw Jira tools, or direct raw JQL access to agents or end users.
items.title: Expose a governed Jira capability surface instead of raw Jira operations
items.rationale: This is stated directly in the project summary and governed fronting intent, including safe denial of raw tool access.

items.body: The product shall support bounded Jira read capabilities for backlog context search and single-issue context retrieval. Read results must be limited to actor-visible fields and bounded context, including optional comments and history where allowed, and must avoid unbounded issue export.
items.title: Return bounded, actor-visible Jira context for read flows
items.rationale: The source explicitly calls for governed Jira reads, bounded context, actor-visible fields, and no unbounded export.

items.body: For governed Jira capabilities, the product shall require clarification when required inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved through backend catalogs. It shall not guess project keys, issue references, statuses, assignees, sprint targets, releases, or link types.
items.title: Require clarification for missing, ambiguous, or unresolved required inputs
items.rationale: The governance posture and input contracts repeatedly specify clarify-on-missing and clarify-on-ambiguous behavior.

items.body: The product shall support preview preparation flows for incident bugs, stories, subtasks, and customer escalation comments. These flows must produce drafts or previews for review without directly creating Jira issues or posting comments as part of the initial request.
items.title: Support preview-only preparation for issue and comment creation flows
items.rationale: The source repeatedly defines these capabilities as prepare-only, write-adjacent, and non-mutating at request time.

items.body: The product shall support governed request flows for workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links. These flows must stop at an approval boundary when policy requires it, returning a preview of the intended change and the stated business reason before any execution occurs.
items.title: Gate Jira-changing requests behind explicit approval boundaries
items.rationale: Approval-required posture is explicit for these change requests, along with preview and approval-stop behavior.

items.body: The product shall explicitly deny requests for raw Jira tool access, raw JQL exposure, unrestricted export, or other out-of-policy operations. Denial responses should be intentional and explain that only governed capabilities are available.
items.title: Deny unsafe or out-of-policy Jira access explicitly
items.rationale: Safe denial of raw tool access and non-exposure of raw Jira/JQL are central goals in the brief.

items.body: The product shall preserve audit evidence for governed Jira interactions, including what capability was invoked, what bounded scope or target was requested, whether the result was a read, clarification, preview, approval stop, or denial, and enough context for later reviewer inspection.
items.title: Produce audit evidence for governed reads, previews, approvals, and denials
items.rationale: Audit evidence is named in the project summary and supported by the governance posture and reviewer actor context.

items.body: The product shall support release-note preparation from bounded Jira issue and release context. It must generate draft release notes for supported audiences without publishing externally and without mutating Jira.
items.title: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issue context without publishing
items.rationale: Release-note preparation is explicitly listed as a capability with read-only, draft-producing behavior.

items.body: The product shall resolve business references such as projects, issues, workflow statuses, sprints, assignees, releases, components, and issue link types through governed backend resolution rather than freeform execution. When resolution fails or is ambiguous, the system must clarify or deny according to policy.
items.title: Resolve business references through governed Jira catalogs
items.rationale: This behavior is strongly implied by the reviewed capability input contracts across nearly all operations.

items.body: The product shall support distinct actor postures for triage, delivery coordination, release preparation, change approval, and audit review. Permissions must distinguish bounded access, approval-required actions, direct audit review access where intended, and denied governance-control access for non-review actors.
items.title: Apply role-aware governance across bounded reads, change requests, and audit review
items.rationale: The in-flight design context provides concrete actor and permission drafts that align with the business brief, but they are not primary source truth.

watchouts: The source mixes accepted intent with starter-template evidence; treat template-suggested operations as draft until verified against the real integration.
Several prepare/request flows reference create or mutation backends, but the stated posture is preview-only or approval-required; execution boundaries must stay explicit.
Bounded context and denial of raw JQL/tool access are core product constraints; avoid later requirements that reintroduce unrestricted search or export.
Catalog-backed resolution is assumed for projects, issues, statuses, sprints, users, releases, components, and link types; unresolved or ambiguous references must clarify rather than guess.
Audit evidence is called out as a product goal, but retention scope and reviewer needs are not yet fully specified.
next_steps: Review and accept, edit, or reject the candidate requirement blocks individually.
Confirm the exact approval boundary for governed change requests versus preview-only preparation flows.
Define the minimum audit record expected for reads, denials, clarifications, previews, and approvals.
Validate which template capabilities are required in the first showcase release versus later expansion.
Verify the real backend seam and endpoint behavior before locking PM language that implies implementation specifics.
questions_for_user: Should approval-gated requests stop at preview only in this showcase, or should approved execution also be modeled in a later phase?
Which Jira projects, boards, releases, and user directories are considered in-scope catalogs for backend resolution?
What audit evidence must be retained for denials, clarifications, previews, approvals, and actor-visible reads?
Should release note preparation support only internal/customer/executive audiences, or are additional audience variants needed?
Do customer-visible comment previews require extra policy checks or redaction rules beyond the stated visibility flag?

envelope.title: Scenario Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Showcase
envelope.summary: Candidate PM scenarios derived from the Jira governed fronting brief. They focus on bounded reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated change requests, denial of raw Jira access, and audit review.

items.body: A triage operator asks for backlog context within an allowed Jira project. The system returns bounded issue context and refuses to expose raw Jira tools or unbounded query behavior.
items.title: Search bounded Jira backlog context without exposing raw JQL
items.rationale: Directly supported by the brief and capability evidence for bounded backlog search, clarification on missing inputs, and denial of raw Jira/JQL access.

scenario.name: search-bounded-jira-backlog-context-without-exposing-raw-jql
scenario.narrative: A Jira triage operator needs to find relevant work in an allowed project. They provide a project and search intent, and the system returns bounded backlog context rather than raw Jira query access or unbounded exports.
scenario.time_scope: ad hoc triage and recurring backlog review
scenario.actor_context: jira_triage_operator reviewing bounded Jira context in an allowed project
expected_behavior: Return bounded issue context for an allowed project.
Require clarification when project scope or query is missing or ambiguous.
Do not expose raw JQL or raw Jira tool access.
Do not return unbounded issue export behavior.

orchestration_steps.action: The actor asks for backlog context for a specific Jira project and search intent.
The fronting service validates required inputs such as project scope and query text.
If inputs are complete, the service performs a bounded backlog search and shapes the result to actor-visible issue context.
If the actor asks for raw JQL, raw Jira tools, or unbounded export behavior, the system denies that path and redirects to governed search.

expected_anip_support: Expose a governed read capability for backlog context search.
Enforce bounded result shaping and actor-visible scope.
Produce explicit denial outcomes for raw Jira or raw JQL requests.
Capture audit evidence for the governed read request.

items.body: A triage operator asks for issue context on a specific Jira issue. The system returns bounded fields, comments, and optional history without exposing raw issue APIs.
items.title: Inspect one Jira issue with bounded comments and history
items.rationale: Directly supported by the issue context capability, including bounded fields, comments, changelog options, and clarification on missing issue reference.

scenario.name: inspect-one-jira-issue-with-bounded-comments-and-history
scenario.narrative: A Jira triage operator needs context on a single issue before deciding next action. They request issue details, and the system returns bounded actor-visible fields with comments and, when requested, history.
scenario.actor_context: jira_triage_operator inspecting a known Jira issue
expected_behavior: Return bounded issue details rather than raw Jira payloads.
Include comments by default.
Include changelog only when requested or otherwise configured.
Clarify missing or ambiguous issue references.

orchestration_steps.action: The actor provides an issue reference and asks for issue context.
The fronting service resolves the issue reference and checks whether comments or changelog were requested.
The service returns bounded issue details, comments by default, and changelog when explicitly requested.
If the issue reference is missing or ambiguous, the system stops for clarification.

expected_anip_support: Expose a governed issue context capability.
Resolve issue references through governed catalogs rather than free-form backend access.
Keep the result actor-visible and bounded.
Record audit evidence for the read path.

items.body: A triage or delivery actor prepares a bug, story, or subtask draft. The system builds a preview and stops before creation, requiring explicit inputs and avoiding silent issue creation.
items.title: Prepare a Jira issue preview before any issue is created
items.rationale: Strongly supported by the prepare-only capabilities for incident bug, story, and subtask, all of which produce previews and stop before mutation.

scenario.name: prepare-a-jira-issue-preview-before-any-issue-is-created
scenario.narrative: An operator wants help drafting Jira work from incident, delivery, or parent-issue context. The system prepares a preview for a bug, story, or subtask and stops before any issue is created.
scenario.actor_context: jira_triage_operator or jira_delivery_coordinator preparing Jira work items
expected_behavior: Support preview-only preparation for bug, story, and subtask flows.
Require clarification for missing required inputs instead of guessing.
Return a draft or preview suitable for review.
Do not create the issue as part of the preparation scenario.

orchestration_steps.action: The actor asks to draft a bug, story, or subtask and provides the required business inputs for that work type.
The fronting service validates required fields such as project, summary, description, acceptance criteria, or parent issue depending on the requested draft type.
The service prepares an issue creation preview using governed metadata and returns the draft for review.
The flow stops at preview; no Jira issue is created through this scenario.

expected_anip_support: Expose governed prepare-only capabilities for issue drafting.
Represent preview outputs as explicit write-adjacent results.
Apply one-time or session-bound grant posture where relevant.
Capture audit evidence that the flow stopped before mutation.

items.body: A triage operator drafts a customer-safe comment for a Jira issue. The system prepares the comment preview with purpose and visibility controls, then stops before posting.
items.title: Prepare a customer escalation comment without posting it
items.rationale: Directly supported by the customer escalation comment preparation capability, including purpose, visibility, and preview-only behavior.

scenario.name: prepare-a-customer-escalation-comment-without-posting-it
scenario.narrative: A Jira triage operator needs to communicate status or impact on an issue but must avoid posting directly. The system prepares a customer-safe comment preview with declared purpose and visibility, then stops before posting.
scenario.actor_context: jira_triage_operator preparing incident or escalation communication
expected_behavior: Prepare a comment preview rather than posting directly.
Require a declared comment purpose and context.
Default visibility to internal unless another allowed value is explicitly chosen.
Clarify missing issue reference or purpose.

orchestration_steps.action: The actor identifies the Jira issue and states the comment purpose and context.
The fronting service validates the issue reference, purpose, and visibility choice.
The service returns a comment preview shaped for the requested visibility.
The flow stops before any Jira comment is posted.

expected_anip_support: Expose a governed comment preparation capability.
Enforce preview-only behavior for write-adjacent comment flows.
Support visibility and purpose controls in the runtime contract.
Record audit evidence for the prepared comment request.

items.body: A delivery coordinator requests a workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, or issue link. The system prepares the change preview, captures the reason, and stops at the approval boundary.
items.title: Prepare a governed Jira change request and stop for approval
items.rationale: Directly supported by the approval-required request capabilities and the project summary emphasizing approval-gated changes.

scenario.name: prepare-a-governed-jira-change-request-and-stop-for-approval
scenario.narrative: A delivery coordinator needs to change Jira state in a governed way. The system prepares a workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, or issue link request, shows the preview, and stops for approval before execution.
scenario.time_scope: during delivery coordination and change control
scenario.actor_context: jira_delivery_coordinator preparing governed Jira changes; jira_change_approver reviews approval-required requests
expected_behavior: Support governed previews for workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link requests.
Require a business reason for the requested change.
Stop for approval before any Jira mutation occurs.
Clarify unresolved or ambiguous references instead of guessing.

orchestration_steps.action: The actor requests a Jira state change such as transition, sprint move, assignee change, or issue link and provides the business reason.
The governance service resolves referenced issues, users, statuses, sprints, or link types through governed catalogs.
The service prepares a preview of the requested change and identifies that approval is required.
The flow stops at the approval boundary; execution is not performed in this scenario.

expected_anip_support: Expose approval-required change request capabilities separate from raw execution.
Use governed resolution for statuses, sprints, assignees, and link types.
Represent approval stop as an explicit runtime outcome.
Capture audit evidence for the requested change and approval boundary.

items.body: A release preparer asks for release notes for a project release. The system gathers bounded issue context, drafts release notes for the intended audience, and does not publish or mutate Jira.
items.title: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing
items.rationale: Directly supported by the release notes preparation capability and the brief's emphasis on preview-only, non-mutating preparation.

scenario.name: prepare-release-notes-from-bounded-jira-issues-without-publishing
scenario.narrative: A release preparer needs a draft summary of work for a Jira release. The system gathers bounded issue context for the specified release and query, then returns release notes tailored to the requested audience without publishing anything.
expected_behavior: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issue context.
Support audience shaping such as internal, customer, or executive when explicitly requested.
Clarify missing project, release, or query inputs.
Do not publish release notes or mutate Jira.

orchestration_steps.action: The actor provides project scope, release reference, and issue query for the release notes draft.
The fronting service resolves the release reference and validates the requested audience and limit.
The service gathers bounded issue context and produces a release notes draft.
The flow ends with a draft only; no publishing or Jira mutation occurs.

expected_anip_support: Expose a governed release note preparation capability.
Keep the output as draft content rather than external dispatch.
Enforce bounded issue retrieval and aggregation.
Capture audit evidence for the release note preparation request.

items.body: A user asks for raw Jira REST, MCP, or raw JQL access. The system explicitly denies the request and points the user to the governed capability surface instead.
items.title: Deny raw Jira tool access and redirect to governed capabilities
items.rationale: Explicitly supported by the fronting intent, project summary, and user-provided brief calling for safe denial of raw tool access.

scenario.name: deny-raw-jira-tool-access-and-redirect-to-governed-capabilities
scenario.narrative: A user attempts to bypass the governed Jira surface by asking for raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or raw JQL. The system denies the request, explains the boundary, and redirects the user to supported governed capabilities.
scenario.time_scope: whenever a raw tool access request is made
scenario.actor_context: any actor interacting with the Jira governed fronting surface
expected_behavior: Do not expose raw Jira REST or MCP operations to the agent.
Do not allow raw JQL as a direct tool surface.
Return an explicit denial rather than silently failing or partially complying.
Redirect the user toward supported governed capabilities where possible.
scenario.primary_capability: No direct raw capability is exposed; denial is part of the governed fronting behavior

orchestration_steps.action: The actor asks for raw Jira REST, MCP, raw JQL, or direct tool execution.
The governed surface detects that the request falls outside the allowed capability set.
The system returns an explicit denial and suggests the closest supported governed capability.
The denial is recorded as audit evidence.

expected_anip_support: Represent denial outcomes explicitly in the runtime contract.
Separate governed capabilities from hidden backend operations.
Capture audit evidence for denied raw access attempts.
Preserve policy boundaries consistently across actors.

items.body: An audit reviewer inspects why a Jira request completed, clarified, was denied, or stopped for approval. The system provides evidence of the governed path taken.
items.title: Review audit evidence for governed Jira reads, previews, denials, and approval stops
items.rationale: Audit evidence is explicit in the project summary and brief, but the exact evidence fields are not fully specified, so the scenario is concrete but still needs confirmation.

scenario.name: review-audit-evidence-for-governed-jira-outcomes
scenario.narrative: An audit reviewer needs to verify that Jira access and change preparation followed the governed surface. They inspect evidence showing what capability was requested, what boundary applied, and whether the result was a bounded read, preview, denial, clarification, or approval stop.
scenario.actor_context: jira_audit_reviewer reviewing governance and audit controls
expected_behavior: Provide reviewable evidence for governed Jira interactions.
Show why a request was allowed, clarified, denied, or stopped for approval.
Support audit review without exposing raw backend secrets or tokens.
Preserve traceability across reads, previews, and approval-gated requests.
scenario.primary_capability: Audit review behavior is supported by the governed fronting surface, but the exact audit retrieval capability is not named in the brief

orchestration_steps.action: The reviewer selects a prior governed Jira interaction for inspection.
The system presents the requested capability, resolved scope, and resulting outcome type.
The reviewer sees whether the flow completed as a bounded read, returned a preview, required clarification, was denied, or stopped for approval.
The reviewer uses the evidence to confirm that raw Jira access was not exposed and that governance posture was preserved.

expected_anip_support: Capture audit evidence as part of governed runtime behavior.
Make outcome type and policy boundary visible for review.
Keep secret posture intact by storing references rather than tokens.
Support PM-visible auditability even if the exact audit retrieval interface is finalized later.

watchouts: The source supports preview and approval-request behavior, but not autonomous execution after approval.
Raw Jira REST, MCP operations, and raw JQL exposure should be framed as denied or hidden from the agent, not as user-facing capabilities.
Several capabilities require clarification on missing or ambiguous inputs; scenarios should show stop conditions instead of inferred defaults.
The actor IDs in the brief are useful context, but only some actor-to-capability mappings are explicit enough to use confidently.
next_steps: Select which candidate scenarios belong in the first PM draft.
Decide whether to keep issue preparation as one consolidated scenario or split by bug, story, subtask, and comment preview.
Confirm the desired audit evidence fields so the audit scenario can be tightened.
After scenario selection, align each accepted scenario to actor permissions and approval boundaries.
questions_for_user: Should issue creation previews be modeled as separate PM scenarios for bug, story, and subtask, or combined into one preparation scenario family?
Do you want customer escalation comment preparation treated as a core showcase scenario or as a secondary example?
Should approval and execution remain out of scope for PM scenarios, with scenarios stopping at preview or approval request only?
For audit review, what evidence must be visible to the reviewer: requested capability, resolved inputs, denial reason, approval stop, and backend operation trace?

envelope.title: Service Design Proposal: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3
envelope.summary: Candidate developer-facing service design blocks derived from the locked Jira governed fronting baseline. Public service design stays focused on the governed capability surface and excludes internal adapter capabilities from the product-facing shape.

items.body: Model the public governed surface as two services. `jira-fronting` handles bounded Jira reads plus preview-only preparation flows. `jira-governance` handles approval-gated change requests, denial of raw Jira/JQL/tool access, and audit-facing governance evidence. Internal adapter execution remains implementation evidence, not public service design.
items.title: Public governed Jira service shape
items.rationale: The source explicitly declares `jira-fronting` and `jira-governance` as the public capability owners, while `jira-adapter` is internal and should not appear as public governed capability surface.

shape.name: Jira Governed Fronting Public Surface
notes: Public service design only; excludes internal adapter/backend seams.
Service boundaries follow source-declared public capability ownership.
Two-service shape fits the balanced topology preference while respecting governed fronting constraints.

services.name: Jira Fronting
responsibilities: Provide bounded Jira context reads without exposing raw Jira REST, MCP, or raw JQL tools.
Prepare preview-only issue and comment actions without executing mutations.
Return clarification stops when required inputs are missing or ambiguous.
Produce bounded release-note preparation outputs from actor-visible Jira data.

services.name: Jira Governance
responsibilities: Own approval-gated Jira change request capabilities before any mutation boundary is crossed.
Enforce denial of raw Jira, raw JQL, and raw tool access at the governed surface.
Provide audit-visible governance evidence for approvals, denials, and request handling.
Apply policy checks for workflow, sprint, assignee, and issue-link changes.

coordination.description: Preview-only preparation and bounded reads stay in `jira-fronting`; approval-gated change requests and control decisions route to `jira-governance` when policy or mutation risk requires it.
Governance outcomes return approval-stop, denial, or audit-linked response context that the fronting experience can present to the actor.

items.body: Keep public capability ownership aligned to the source evidence: bounded reads and prepare-only previews on `jira-fronting`; approval-required requests on `jira-governance`. Add only two inferred public control capabilities if needed: one for explicit denial of raw Jira/JQL/tool access and one for audit evidence retrieval/review.
items.title: Capability ownership and public surface review notes
items.rationale: Most capability ids are source-declared. The denial and audit behaviors are clearly required by the brief, but their canonical public ids are not explicitly declared in the source.

items.body: Do not publish `jira-adapter` or any `jira.adapter.*` capability ids in Product Service Design. Those ids represent internal read/resolve/execute seams and belong in Developer Design backend evidence that maps the governed surface to native Jira API or MCP implementations.
items.title: Keep adapter and backend execution out of public service design
items.rationale: The source explicitly includes adapter capabilities as developer evidence, but governed fronting guidance says the agent must see governed capabilities, not raw Jira REST or MCP operations.

watchouts: This is a governed fronting project, so public Product Service Design should not expose internal adapter capabilities such as `jira.adapter.*`.

The source mentions audit evidence and denial of raw tool access, but no explicit public capability ids are declared for those behaviors; any added ids below are inferred and need PM/dev confirmation.
`jira-fronting` owns both read-only context and prepare-only preview flows; confirm that this mixed posture is intentional rather than a future split candidate.
Approval-required change requests are clearly separated into `jira-governance`; keep capability ownership unique and avoid duplicating request capabilities on `jira-fronting`.
If raw access denial must be externally callable, the inferred deny capability id should be reviewed carefully because the source only states the behavior, not the canonical id.
next_steps: Confirm the public service count and whether `jira-adapter` stays out of Product Service Design.
Approve or edit the inferred public capability ids for denial and audit evidence before formal contract drafting.
After service ownership is accepted, derive capability contracts, approval stops, and audit bindings per service.
Use Developer Design backend evidence to map these public services to native Jira API or MCP seams without exposing raw operations publicly.
questions_for_user: Should Product Service Design model two public services (`jira-fronting` and `jira-governance`) and leave `jira-adapter` to Developer Design backend evidence only?
Do you want explicit deny behavior for raw Jira/JQL/tool access represented as a public capability, or only as a governance rule documented on existing services?
Should audit evidence be modeled as a separate public governed capability surface, or as cross-cutting behavior owned by `jira-governance`?
Are the source service ids `jira-fronting` and `jira-governance` intended to be canonical public service ids for this package?

envelope.title: Non-Goal Patch Candidates: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3
envelope.summary: PM-facing non-goal candidates derived from the Jira governed fronting brief. These focus on preserving bounded reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated changes, denial of raw Jira access, and audit-oriented governance boundaries.

value.rationale: The brief repeatedly states that the agent must see governed Jira capabilities rather than native operations, and that raw tool access should be safely denied.
value.statement: Do not expose raw Jira REST, MCP, or raw tool access to agents or end users.

patches.rationale: Makes the fronting boundary explicit and prevents scope drift into direct tool passthrough.

value.rationale: The backlog and issue-context capabilities are defined as bounded, actor-visible reads without raw JQL exposure or unbounded export.
value.statement: Do not support raw JQL entry or unbounded Jira issue export.

patches.rationale: Protects the governed-read posture and avoids turning the showcase into a generic Jira query/export surface.

value.rationale: Write-adjacent actions are described as prepare-only or approval-required, with explicit stops before execution.
value.statement: Do not create, transition, assign, link, comment on, or move Jira issues automatically without the intended preview or approval stop.

patches.rationale: Prevents silent automation from undermining the approval-gated product intent.

value.rationale: The governance posture explicitly says missing required inputs should trigger clarification rather than guessing.
value.statement: Do not guess missing required inputs for Jira actions; require clarification instead.

patches.rationale: Keeps the product aligned with safe, reviewable operation contracts.

value.rationale: Release-note capability scope is limited to preparation from bounded Jira issues and explicitly excludes publishing or mutation.
value.statement: Do not publish or distribute release notes automatically; only prepare bounded drafts.

patches.rationale: Avoids expanding the showcase into outbound publishing workflows not supported by the brief.

value.rationale: Reads are defined as bounded and actor-visible, and the project centers on governed Jira context rather than broad data retrieval.
value.statement: Do not return Jira data beyond bounded actor-visible context.

patches.rationale: Reinforces least-visibility behavior as a product boundary, not just a backend detail.

value.rationale: The project summary explicitly includes audit evidence as part of the canonical governed showcase.
value.statement: Do not treat audit evidence as optional or secondary to convenience automation.

patches.rationale: Preserves auditability as a core boundary when future feature pressure pushes toward faster direct actions.

watchouts: Several source documents are starter-template guidance, so non-goals should stay anchored to the explicit project brief and repeated governance posture.
The brief says raw Jira, JQL, REST, and MCP operations should not be exposed to agents; this should be stated as a durable boundary, not just an implementation detail.
Prepare/request capabilities reference backend create or mutation operations, but the intended product boundary is still preview-only or approval-gated rather than autonomous execution.
Avoid writing non-goals that conflict with the accepted scope for audit evidence, governed reads, and approval-required change requests.
next_steps: Review which boundaries should be explicit first-release non-goals versus implementation notes.
Confirm whether admin tooling, policy authoring, and compliance reporting are intentionally out of scope.
Accept the patch candidates that best lock the product boundary before persistence.
questions_for_user: Should audit evidence remain review-only, or should the product also avoid building full compliance reporting workflows in this phase?
Do you want to explicitly exclude any direct end-user admin surface for configuring Jira policies, catalogs, or approval rules?
Should release-note preparation be limited to draft generation only, with no publishing or outbound distribution in scope?

envelope.title: Success Criteria Patch Proposal: Jira governed fronting showcase
envelope.summary: PM-facing patch candidates focused on proving the Jira showcase delivers governed reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated change requests, explicit denial of raw Jira access, and audit-ready evidence.

value.evidence: Scenario review shows backlog search and issue context flows return actor-visible bounded results, while requests for raw Jira or raw query/tool access are explicitly denied or redirected to governed capabilities.
value.statement: Users can retrieve bounded Jira backlog or issue context through governed capabilities without being exposed to raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL-style access.

patches.rationale: The brief centers on governed Jira reads, bounded context, and safe denial of raw tool access.

value.evidence: Representative flow review shows write-adjacent actions stop at preview or approval request states and do not execute direct Jira changes without the required approval boundary.
value.statement: Issue, subtask, bug, story, customer comment, workflow, sprint, assignee, and issue-link flows produce previews or approval requests before any Jira mutation is allowed.

patches.rationale: The source repeatedly specifies preview-only preparation and approval-gated changes as core product intent.

value.evidence: Review scenarios across search, issue lookup, assignee resolution, and change-request flows show explicit clarification prompts for missing or ambiguous project, issue, user, sprint, status, or relationship inputs.
value.statement: When required inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved, the product asks for clarification instead of guessing or silently failing.

patches.rationale: The governance posture explicitly requires clarification rather than guessing.

value.evidence: PM review of workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link request examples shows the target change, affected Jira items, and stated reason are visible at approval time.
value.statement: Approval-required Jira change requests provide enough preview detail and business reason context for a change approver to review the intended action before execution.

patches.rationale: The brief calls for approval-gated changes and the evidence shows reason-bearing request flows.

value.evidence: Audit review can inspect traceable records for representative read, denial, preview, approval-stop, and approved-action scenarios without reconstructing the flow from raw backend logs alone.
value.statement: The showcase produces reviewable audit evidence for governed reads, denials, previews, approvals, and approved change execution paths.

patches.rationale: Audit evidence is explicitly named in the project summary and user brief.

value.evidence: Scenario review shows release-note preparation returns a draft artifact based on bounded Jira inputs and does not trigger Jira updates or external dispatch.
value.statement: Release-note preparation produces a bounded draft from Jira issue and release context without publishing externally or mutating Jira.

patches.rationale: Release-note preparation is a named capability with read-only, draft-producing intent.

watchouts: The source is template-derived in places, so criteria should stay at the governed product-behavior level rather than overcommitting to backend implementation details.
Several write flows are preview-only or approval-required; success criteria should not imply direct mutation by the agent.
Audit evidence is called out in the brief, but the exact evidence artifact is not specified.
Release-note preparation is read-only drafting, so success criteria should avoid implying publishing or outbound distribution.
next_steps: Confirm whether PM wants one consolidated showcase criterion set or separate criteria by business area.
Decide the minimum review scenarios needed to prove bounded reads, preview preparation, approval stops, denials, and audit evidence.
After acceptance, convert the selected patch candidates into the Success Criteria section.
questions_for_user: Should success be measured across all listed Jira capabilities, or only the showcase-critical subset?
What evidence format should PM treat as sufficient for audit evidence: scenario traces, approval logs, or both?
Do you want a separate success criterion for explicit denial of raw Jira REST, MCP, and JQL requests?

bundle.sourceText:
# Source 1: Jira developer evidence
Kind: API docs
Filename: jira-developer-evidence.template.md

# Jira Developer Evidence

Imported from starter template `jira-fronting-starter`. This is developer-owned evidence for Studio Autopilot and generated-service review.

This document turns starter operations into explicit ANIP runtime governance and input-contract evidence. Review it against the real integration before publishing.

## Capability Evidence Summary

| capability_id | service_id | operation_type | side_effect_level | inputs | backend_operation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | ---: | --- |
| jira.backlog.search_context | jira-fronting | read | read | 6 | jira.rest.search_issues |
| jira.issue.get_context | jira-fronting | read | read | 4 | jira.rest.get_issue; jira.rest.get_comments; jira.rest.get_changelog |
| jira.incident_bug.prepare | jira-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 7 | jira.rest.get_create_metadata; jira.rest.create_issue |
| jira.story.prepare | jira-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 6 | jira.rest.get_create_metadata; jira.rest.create_issue |
| jira.subtask.prepare | jira-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 5 | jira.rest.get_issue; jira.rest.create_issue |
| jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare | jira-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 5 | jira.rest.get_issue; jira.rest.add_comment |
| jira.workflow_transition.request | jira-governance | write | approval_required | 5 | jira.rest.get_transitions; jira.rest.transition_issue |
| jira.sprint_move.request | jira-governance | write | approval_required | 4 | jira.rest.get_boards; jira.rest.get_sprints; jira.rest.move_issues_to_sprint |
| jira.assignee_change.request | jira-governance | write | approval_required | 4 | jira.rest.assign_issue; jira.rest.user_search |
| jira.issue_link.request | jira-governance | write | approval_required | 5 | jira.rest.get_issue_link_types; jira.rest.link_issues |
| jira.release_notes.prepare | jira-fronting | read | read | 6 | jira.rest.search_issues; jira.rest.get_versions |
| jira.adapter.read.issue_context | jira-adapter | read | read | 1 | jira.rest.get_issue; jira.rest.get_comments; jira.rest.get_changelog |
| jira.adapter.read.backlog_context | jira-adapter | read | read | 2 | jira.rest.search_issues |
| jira.adapter.resolve.assignee | jira-adapter | read | read | 1 | jira.rest.user_search |
| jira.adapter.execute.workflow_transition | jira-adapter | write | approval_required | 2 | jira.rest.transition_issue |
| jira.adapter.execute.sprint_move | jira-adapter | write | approval_required | 2 | jira.rest.move_issues_to_sprint |
| jira.adapter.execute.assignee_change | jira-adapter | write | approval_required | 2 | jira.rest.assign_issue |
| jira.adapter.execute.issue_link | jira-adapter | write | approval_required | 3 | jira.rest.link_issues |

## Reviewed Developer Evidence

# Jira Integration Evidence Template

> Imported from starter template `jira-fronting-starter`. Verify every operation against your chosen native API/MCP source before locking.

Native API connection: JIRA_BASE_URL
MCP connection: ATLASSIAN_MCP_ENDPOINT

## Template-suggested backend operations
- jira.rest.search_issues: GET /jira/backlog/context/search
- jira.rest.get_issue: GET /jira/issues/{issue_key}/context
- jira.rest.get_comments: GET /jira/issues/{issue_key}/context
- jira.rest.get_changelog: GET /jira/issues/{issue_key}/context
- jira.rest.get_create_metadata: POST /jira/incidents/bugs/preview
- jira.rest.create_issue: POST /jira/incidents/bugs/preview
- jira.rest.get_create_metadata: POST /jira/stories/preview
- jira.rest.create_issue: POST /jira/stories/preview
- jira.rest.get_issue: POST /jira/issues/{parent_issue_key}/subtasks/preview
- jira.rest.create_issue: POST /jira/issues/{parent_issue_key}/subtasks/preview
- jira.rest.get_issue: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/customer-escalation-comment/preview
- jira.rest.add_comment: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/customer-escalation-comment/preview
- jira.rest.get_transitions: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/workflow-transition/request
- jira.rest.transition_issue: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/workflow-transition/request
- jira.rest.get_boards: POST /jira/sprints/move-request
- jira.rest.get_sprints: POST /jira/sprints/move-request
- jira.rest.move_issues_to_sprint: POST /jira/sprints/move-request
- jira.rest.assign_issue: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/assignee-change/request
- jira.rest.user_search: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/assignee-change/request
- jira.rest.get_issue_link_types: POST /jira/issues/link-request
- jira.rest.link_issues: POST /jira/issues/link-request
- jira.rest.search_issues: GET /jira/releases/{release_ref}/notes/preview
- jira.rest.get_versions: GET /jira/releases/{release_ref}/notes/preview

## Secret posture
- Studio stores secret references only; project exports must not contain tokens.
- Teams can implement either native API or MCP backend seams behind the same governed ANIP contract.
