# Source 3: Jira governed fronting intent
Kind: Fronting intent
Filename: jira-fronting-intent.md

# Jira Governed Fronting Intent

> Imported from starter template `jira-fronting-starter`. Treat this as draft guidance, not discovered truth.

Expose a governed Jira capability surface in front of native API or MCP-backed operations.

## Template-suggested capabilities
- jira.backlog.search_context: Find bounded work context in an allowed Jira project without exposing raw JQL or unbounded issue export.
- jira.issue.get_context: Inspect one Jira issue with bounded actor-visible fields, comments, and history.
- jira.incident_bug.prepare: Prepare a Jira bug ticket from incident or defect context without creating it.
- jira.story.prepare: Prepare a Jira story preview with acceptance criteria before any issue is created.
- jira.subtask.prepare: Prepare a subtask under an existing parent issue without creating it.
- jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare: Prepare a customer-safe Jira comment from incident or triage context without posting it.
- jira.workflow_transition.request: Request a governed Jira issue transition and stop for approval when policy requires it.
- jira.sprint_move.request: Request moving Jira issue(s) into a sprint or backlog with approval and actor-visible board scope.
- jira.assignee_change.request: Request reassignment of a Jira issue with backend user resolution, preview, approval, and audit.
- jira.issue_link.request: Request linking two Jira issues with a declared relationship and cross-project policy checks.
- jira.release_notes.prepare: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing or mutating Jira.

## Governance posture
- Raw Jira tools are not exposed directly to agents.
- Reads return bounded actor-visible context.
- Write-adjacent actions prepare previews or require explicit approval before execution.
- Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessing.

## User-provided project brief
Create the canonical Jira governed fronting showcase. The ANIP service must expose business capabilities for bounded Jira context, issue preparation, workflow and sprint-change previews, assignee-change requests, issue-link requests, release-note preparation, approval-required write-adjacent actions, explicit denials for raw Jira/JQL/tool access, and audit evidence. The agent must see governed capabilities, not raw Jira REST or MCP operations.

business_goals: Enable bounded, actor-visible Jira context retrieval for backlog and issue review.
Let users prepare Jira issue creation, comments, release notes, and change requests as previews before execution.
Require explicit approval for governed Jira changes such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links.
Deny direct raw Jira, raw JQL, and raw tool access in favor of governed business capabilities.
Preserve audit evidence for governed reads, previews, requests, and approvals.
data.product_purpose: Provide a governed Jira fronting experience that lets users retrieve bounded Jira context and prepare reviewable Jira changes without exposing raw Jira tools or bypassing approval controls.
data.business_problem: Teams need Jira access and action support, but direct exposure to raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or raw JQL can create unsafe scope, inconsistent review, weak auditability, and uncontrolled mutations.
data.approval_posture_summary: Read-only context retrieval can proceed within bounded scope, while write-adjacent actions either stop at preview or require explicit approval before any Jira mutation is executed. Higher-impact changes are approval-gated and auditable.
data.governed_behavior_summary: The product exposes governed Jira capabilities instead of raw Jira operations. Reads return bounded actor-visible context, write-adjacent actions produce previews or requests rather than direct execution, missing required inputs trigger clarification, and unsafe raw access requests are explicitly denied.

actors.notes: Derived from the brief’s emphasis on bounded reads, issue preparation, customer-safe comment preparation, explicit denial of raw tool access, and clarification instead of guessing.
actors.title: Jira Triage Operator
actors.summary: Operational user who inspects bounded Jira issue and backlog context and prepares issue or comment drafts without direct raw Jira tool access.
actors.action_expectations: Can search backlog context, inspect issue context, prepare incident bugs, prepare subtasks, and prepare customer escalation comments within governed input rules and clarification flows.
actors.approval_expectations: Can initiate preview-oriented and approval-gated requests but should stop at explicit approval boundaries for governed mutations.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can see bounded Jira project, issue, comment, changelog, and backlog context exposed through governed capabilities, plus previews and audit-relevant request context. Should not see raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL/tool access.

actors.notes: This actor groups the brief’s governed change-request capabilities that are operational but not final-execution authority.
actors.title: Jira Delivery Coordinator
actors.summary: Planning-oriented user who prepares and requests governed changes to issue workflow, sprint placement, assignee, and issue relationships.
actors.action_expectations: Can request workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links through governed preview and request flows with stated business reasons.
actors.approval_expectations: Can assemble and submit approval-required change requests, but execution authority is separate unless you confirm this actor is also an approver.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can see actor-visible workflow, sprint, assignee-resolution, and issue-link preview context needed to understand proposed changes, but not raw backend operations or unrestricted Jira administration surfaces.

actors.notes: Kept separate because release-note preparation is a distinct business workflow in the source, even though it is read-only.
actors.title: Jira Release Preparer
actors.summary: Release-focused user who prepares bounded release notes from Jira issue context without publishing or mutating Jira.
actors.action_expectations: Can prepare release notes drafts from governed Jira release and issue queries and tailor drafts for internal, customer, or executive audiences where allowed.
actors.approval_expectations: Can prepare release-note drafts directly; approval or publication authority is not specified in the brief and should be clarified if needed.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can see bounded release and issue context sufficient to draft release notes for an intended audience, without raw export or unrestricted issue extraction.

actors.notes: Use only if your operating model includes a human approval step rather than a purely policy-driven gate.
actors.title: Jira Change Approver
actors.summary: Reviewing actor who evaluates governed Jira change requests before any approval-required mutation proceeds.
actors.action_expectations: Can review workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests and decide whether they should proceed under governance.
actors.approval_expectations: Acts as the explicit approval boundary for approval-required Jira changes, but exact authority by action type is not specified in the source and should be confirmed.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can inspect the proposed change, target issue or sprint context, stated reason, and supporting audit evidence needed to approve or deny governed Jira mutations.

actors.notes: Included because the brief explicitly requires audit evidence and safe denial behavior.
actors.title: Jira Audit Reviewer
actors.summary: Oversight actor who reviews audit evidence for governed Jira access, prepared changes, approvals, and denials.
actors.action_expectations: Can review evidence of bounded reads, prepared write-adjacent actions, approval stops, and explicit denials of raw Jira/JQL/tool access.
actors.approval_expectations: Typically not the operational approver unless your process combines audit and approval responsibilities; this should be clarified.
actors.visibility_expectations: Can inspect audit evidence showing what governed capability was used, what was requested or denied, and where approval boundaries were enforced. Should not gain raw Jira tool access through the audit role.

envelope.title: Reviewed Capability Formalizations
envelope.summary: Extracted 18 reviewed capability formalizations from developer source evidence. Review before saving contract truth.

items.body: Preserved 18 reviewed capability formalizations from developer source evidence.
items.title: Canonical capability formalizations
items.rationale: Extracted directly from developer-owned structured capability-formalization evidence; no model inference was required.

capabilities.title: Search Backlog Context
capabilities.summary: Find bounded work context in an allowed Jira project without exposing raw JQL or unbounded issue export.

capabilities.title: Get Issue Context
capabilities.summary: Inspect one Jira issue with bounded actor-visible fields, comments, and history.

capabilities.title: Prepare Incident Bug
capabilities.summary: Prepare a Jira bug ticket from incident or defect context without creating it.

capabilities.title: Prepare Story
capabilities.summary: Prepare a Jira story preview with acceptance criteria before any issue is created.

capabilities.title: Prepare Subtask
capabilities.summary: Prepare a subtask under an existing parent issue without creating it.

capabilities.title: Prepare Customer Escalation Comment
capabilities.summary: Prepare a customer-safe Jira comment from incident or triage context without posting it.

capabilities.title: Request Workflow Transition
capabilities.summary: Request a governed Jira issue transition and stop for approval when policy requires it.

capabilities.title: Request Sprint Move
capabilities.summary: Request moving Jira issue(s) into a sprint or backlog with approval and actor-visible board scope.

capabilities.title: Request Assignee Change
capabilities.summary: Request reassignment of a Jira issue with backend user resolution, preview, approval, and audit.

capabilities.title: Request Issue Link
capabilities.summary: Request linking two Jira issues with a declared relationship and cross-project policy checks.

capabilities.title: Prepare Release Notes
capabilities.summary: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing or mutating Jira.

capabilities.title: Read Issue Context
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter read for bounded Jira issue fields, comments, and changelog context.

capabilities.title: Read Backlog Context
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter read for bounded Jira backlog search context without raw JQL exposure.

capabilities.title: Resolve Assignee
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter lookup that resolves an assignee reference to a Jira user identity.

capabilities.title: Execute Workflow Transition
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter mutation available only after governed approval for a Jira workflow transition.

capabilities.title: Execute Sprint Move
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter mutation available only after governed approval for moving Jira issues to a sprint.

capabilities.title: Execute Assignee Change
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter mutation available only after governed approval for changing a Jira issue assignee.

capabilities.title: Execute Issue Link
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter mutation available only after governed approval for linking Jira issues.

next_steps: Review the extracted capability formalizations in Capability Formalization.
Save only after the developer-owned interface evidence matches the intended runtime surface.

envelope.title: Runtime Policy Binding Proposal: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3
envelope.summary: Candidate runtime policy binding blocks derived from the locked Jira governed-fronting baseline, service split, and developer evidence. Focus is on governed reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated change requests, explicit denial of raw access, and audit evidence posture.

items.body: Runtime policy should allow only the locked public capability inventory on the public HTTP surface: bounded backlog search, single-issue context retrieval, preview-only issue/comment preparation, release-note preparation, and approval-gated change requests. The policy should explicitly deny raw Jira REST access, raw MCP operations, raw JQL exposure, unrestricted export behavior, and any agent-facing contract that exposes backend tool semantics directly.
items.title: Bind the public runtime surface to governed Jira capabilities only
items.rationale: The baseline and scenarios repeatedly require a governed fronting surface, safe denial of raw tool access, and no exposure of raw backend semantics.

items.body: Runtime policy should treat project keys, issue references, workflow statuses, sprints, assignees, releases, components, and link types as governed references resolved through backend catalogs where defined. If a required input is missing, ambiguous, or unresolved, the runtime outcome should be clarification_required or deny according to the reviewed input contract, and the system must not guess values.
items.title: Require clarification for missing, ambiguous, or unresolved business references
items.rationale: The locked requirements and developer evidence are explicit that governed Jira flows must clarify rather than guess, with per-input resolution behavior already drafted.

items.body: Runtime policy should bind jira.backlog.search_context, jira.issue.get_context, and jira.release_notes.prepare to bounded, actor-visible Jira data only. Search results should remain scoped to allowed project context and bounded limits; issue context should return bounded fields with comments by default and changelog only when requested or configured; release-note preparation should aggregate bounded issue context without publishing or mutating Jira.
items.title: Constrain read capabilities to actor-visible bounded context
items.rationale: The baseline defines bounded reads, actor-visible fields, no unbounded export, and non-mutating release-note preparation.

items.body: Runtime policy should mark jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, and jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare as prepare_only flows that may gather metadata or context behind the boundary but must stop at a draft or preview outcome. These bindings should prohibit direct issue creation or comment posting as part of the initial request and should preserve any one-time or session-bound delegation posture used for preview preparation.
items.title: Bind issue and comment preparation flows to preview-only outcomes
items.rationale: Both the baseline and developer evidence define these capabilities as preview-only, write-adjacent, and non-mutating at the public contract.

items.body: Runtime policy should mark jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, and jira.issue_link.request as approval-gated request flows. Each request should require a stated business reason, produce a preview of the intended change, apply policy checks before any backend mutation boundary, and return an explicit approval_stop outcome whenever approval is required.
items.title: Bind change request capabilities to explicit approval-stop behavior
items.rationale: The locked baseline and scenarios consistently describe these as governed request flows that stop for approval before execution.

items.body: Runtime policy should distinguish actor postures for triage, delivery coordination, release preparation, change approval, and audit review. Triage and delivery actors should be allowed bounded reads and relevant preview preparation; release preparation should be allowed release-note drafting; change approvers should be the posture eligible to review approval-gated requests; audit reviewers should have review access to governance evidence; non-review actors should be denied governance-control access not intended for them.
items.title: Apply role-aware runtime posture across reads, preparation, approval, and audit review
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires role-aware governance, but principal names and exact principal-to-role mapping are not yet locked.

items.body: Runtime policy should require durable and searchable audit evidence for governed Jira reads, previews, clarifications, denials, and approval stops. Evidence should capture capability identity, requested bounded scope or target, outcome type, applied policy boundary, and lineage fields including task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id. Secret posture should store references rather than tokens.
items.title: Require durable, searchable audit evidence on all governed outcomes
items.rationale: Audit durability, searchability, lineage, and secret-reference posture are all explicit in the locked baseline and supporting scenarios.

items.body: Runtime policy should preserve the public service split: Jira Fronting owns bounded reads, preview-only preparation, clarification stops, and release-note drafting; Jira Governance owns approval-gated change requests, raw-access denial enforcement at the governed surface, and governance-visible evidence for approvals and denials. Handoffs between the two services should preserve capability identity, governed outcome type, and backend boundary evidence.
items.title: Bind runtime enforcement to the two-service public ownership split
items.rationale: The locked service design defines a canonical two-service public shape with explicit ownership and handoff expectations.

watchouts: The baseline requires denial of raw Jira access, but no canonical public denial capability is named; policy should bind denial behavior without inventing a new public capability.
Audit evidence production is explicit, but the exact retrieval interface is not locked; avoid overcommitting to a retrieval contract.
Approval-gated request capabilities are prepare-only at the public surface; runtime policy must not imply direct mutation from Jira Governance requests.
Clarification, denial, preview, bounded read, and approval stop are distinct governed outcomes and should not collapse into generic error handling.
Actor-visible scope and bounded result shaping are core policy constraints for reads and release-note preparation; avoid accidental raw payload passthrough.
next_steps: Review and accept or edit the candidate blocks below.
Map accepted actor postures to concrete runtime principals and token scopes.
Decide whether approval-required requests are always human-approved or may later support policy auto-approval.
Specify the minimum audit evidence fields required on each governed outcome.
Refine backend option handling so hidden backend seams do not leak into the public contract.
questions_for_user: Should runtime policy bindings name the actor postures explicitly as jira_triage_operator, jira_delivery_coordinator, jira_release_preparer, jira_change_approver, and jira_audit_reviewer, or should they stay generic until principal mapping is finalized?
Should approval-required Jira Governance capabilities always stop for approval, or can some requests auto-approve under narrow policy later?
Do you want a dedicated runtime binding for audit evidence retrieval now, or only evidence production requirements on reads, previews, denials, and approval stops?
Should backend_options remain pass-through but hidden from agent-facing semantics, or be further constrained in runtime policy?
For raw Jira REST, MCP, raw JQL, and unrestricted export requests, should denial responses include suggested governed capability redirects by default?

envelope.title: Backend Binding Proposal: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3
envelope.summary: Candidate backend-binding blocks for the governed Jira public surface. Focus is on explicit downstream Jira binding, service-to-adapter routing, approval-gated execution seams, and audit-safe secret posture.

items.body: For generated backend bindings, map public capabilities to the governed Jira HTTP surface described in integration evidence rather than exposing raw Jira REST semantics. Candidate endpoint bindings include backlog context search, issue context retrieval, issue/comment preview endpoints, workflow transition request, sprint move request, assignee change request, issue link request, and release notes preview. Preserve capability-first contracts at the public boundary while allowing internal translation to Jira REST or MCP behind the seam.
items.title: Bind public capabilities to governed Jira HTTP endpoints, not raw Jira APIs
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly denies raw Jira REST, MCP, and raw JQL exposure while the integration evidence provides governed endpoint-shaped operations for the same capabilities.

items.body: Bind Jira Fronting to bounded read and preview-only preparation capabilities: jira.backlog.search_context, jira.issue.get_context, jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare, and jira.release_notes.prepare. Bind Jira Governance to approval-gated request capabilities: jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, and jira.issue_link.request. Keep this routing explicit in backend bindings so generated code does not blur preview/read behavior with approval control behavior.
items.title: Route backend bindings by public service ownership
items.rationale: The locked service design is canonical for public capability ownership and coordination between Jira Fronting and Jira Governance.

items.body: Model downstream Jira access through an internal adapter seam that performs bounded reads, catalog-backed resolution, and post-approval execution calls. Candidate internal bindings include adapter reads for issue and backlog context, assignee resolution, and execution-only operations for workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link. Treat the adapter as hidden implementation plumbing, not part of the public capability inventory.
items.title: Use an internal Jira adapter seam for downstream Jira operations
items.rationale: Developer evidence names a jira-adapter with concrete read, resolve, and execute operations, while the public service shape intentionally excludes internal adapter seams.

items.body: Backend bindings for prepare and request capabilities should separate preview generation from mutation execution. For jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, and jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare, bindings should allow metadata lookup and draft shaping but stop before create_issue or add_comment execution. For jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, and jira.issue_link.request, bindings should produce a preview plus approval boundary and only permit execution through a distinct approved path.
items.title: Enforce non-mutating preview and approval-stop guardrails in bindings
items.rationale: The baseline repeatedly requires preview-only preparation and approval-gated changes, even though developer evidence references underlying mutating Jira operations.

items.body: Make catalog-backed resolution an explicit dependency in backend bindings for project keys, issue references, workflow statuses, sprints, assignees, releases, components, and link types. Resolution failures or ambiguity should return clarification or denial outcomes according to the capability contract rather than falling through to freeform Jira execution. This should apply consistently across reads, previews, and approval-gated requests.
items.title: Bind entity resolution to governed Jira catalogs before backend execution
items.rationale: The locked baseline requires governed backend resolution and explicitly forbids guessing unresolved business references.

items.body: Every backend binding should emit durable, searchable audit evidence capturing capability invoked, requested scope or target, outcome type, and backend boundary evidence, while propagating task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id. Bindings should use secret references only and avoid storing or exporting Jira tokens. Denials for raw access attempts and approval stops should be recorded with the same lineage model as successful bounded reads and previews.
items.title: Attach audit evidence, lineage, and secret-reference posture to every backend binding
items.rationale: Audit durability, searchability, lineage fields, signed trust mode, and secret-reference posture are all explicit in the locked baseline and integration evidence.

watchouts: The locked baseline says production_single_service, but the locked service shape is multi_service; backend bindings should not hide this topology mismatch.
Public capability contracts must not expose raw Jira REST, raw JQL, or raw tool semantics even if backend bindings use them internally.
Several prepare/request capabilities list mutating Jira operations in developer evidence; bindings must enforce preview-only or approval-stop behavior before any mutation call is allowed.
Audit evidence is required and searchable, but the exact retrieval interface is not named; avoid overcommitting to an audit API shape not present in source.
Secret posture is reference-only; bindings must not assume exported tokens, embedded credentials, or direct secret material in generated artifacts.
If MCP is supported behind the seam, it must remain interchangeable with native Jira HTTP without changing the governed ANIP contract.
next_steps: Confirm whether backend bindings should be expressed against internal jira-adapter seams, direct governed Jira HTTP endpoints, or both as selectable implementations.
Decide the canonical runtime topology for this release: true multi-service public surface or collapsed single deployable with logical service separation.
Specify environment targets and tenant/base URL strategy for Jira Fronting and Jira Governance.
Define the approval execution handoff after approval, especially for workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link execution.
Choose or defer the durable audit evidence sink and lineage propagation requirements for invocation, parent invocation, task, and client reference IDs.
questions_for_user: Should both public services bind to the same downstream Jira tenant/base URL, or do Jira Fronting and Jira Governance target different Jira environments?

Do you want the internal jira-adapter treated as a required implementation seam for all downstream Jira calls, or may public services call the governed Jira HTTP endpoints directly where the integration evidence maps 1:1?

For approval-required flows, should execution remain fully separated from preview/request handling at runtime, with jira-governance only preparing approval artifacts and a distinct post-approval executor invoking the adapter?
What concrete audit store or evidence sink should bindings target for durable, searchable records, or should this remain implementation-open for now?
Should MCP remain an allowed hidden backend option behind the same contract, or should bindings be locked to native Jira HTTP only for this version?

envelope.title: Verification Expectations Proposal: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3
envelope.summary: Candidate developer-facing verification expectation blocks derived from the locked Jira governed fronting baseline, scenario set, and developer evidence. Focus is on proving bounded reads, preview-only preparation, approval stops, denials, and audit evidence without redefining product scope.

items.body: Verification should prove that backlog context search and single-issue context retrieval return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context through the governed surface. Checks should confirm that project, issue, status, and release references are resolved through governed catalogs or clarified when missing, ambiguous, or unresolved. Verification should also confirm that raw JQL is never exposed as the contract, unbounded export behavior is not available, comments are included where expected, and changelog/history is included only when requested or configured.
items.title: Verify bounded Jira read behavior and clarification handling
items.rationale: The baseline and scenarios are explicit about bounded reads, clarification instead of guessing, and denial of raw JQL or unrestricted export. Developer evidence provides concrete inputs and defaults for backlog search and issue context.

items.body: Verification should prove that incident bug, story, subtask, and customer escalation comment capabilities stop at draft or preview output and do not create issues or post comments during the initial request path. Checks should confirm required inputs are enforced, missing inputs trigger clarification, closed-value inputs deny unsupported values, and preview responses preserve governed capability identity rather than backend tool semantics. Where preview generation touches create metadata or issue context, verification should show that the runtime outcome remains write-adjacent only and records evidence that the flow stopped before mutation.
items.title: Verify preview-only issue and comment preparation flows
items.rationale: Preview-only preparation is a core product promise in the baseline and is reinforced by multiple safety scenarios. Developer evidence names the prepare-only posture, required inputs, and non-mutation business effects.

items.body: Verification should prove that workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link requests prepare a governed preview, require a stated business reason, and stop at the approval boundary before any Jira mutation occurs. Checks should confirm governed resolution of statuses, sprints, assignees, issue references, and link types; clarification on ambiguity; denial when unresolved values are out of policy; and preservation of actor-visible scope and policy checks such as cross-project link controls. Verification should also confirm that approval-stop is represented as an explicit outcome rather than an implicit failure or hidden backend hold.
items.title: Verify approval-gated Jira change request behavior
items.rationale: Approval-gated changes are central to the project summary and requirements. The scenario pack and developer evidence provide concrete request capabilities, required reason fields, and approval-required side-effect posture.

items.body: Verification should prove that requests for raw Jira REST, MCP operations, raw Jira tools, raw JQL exposure, unrestricted export, or similar out-of-policy access are denied intentionally and consistently. Checks should confirm the denial explains that only governed capabilities are available, does not leak backend operation names as the supported contract, and redirects the actor toward a governed capability when a valid alternative exists. Verification should treat denial as a first-class runtime outcome with audit evidence, not as a generic error path.
items.title: Verify explicit denial of raw Jira access and unsupported operations
items.rationale: Safe denial of raw tool access is explicit in the project summary, requirements, service responsibilities, and denial scenario. This is a key non-goal guard for the governed fronting design.

items.body: Verification should prove that governed Jira interactions produce durable, searchable evidence for reads, clarifications, previews, approval stops, and denials. At minimum, checks should confirm evidence captures the invoked capability, requested bounded scope or target, outcome type, applied policy boundary, and lineage identifiers such as task, invocation, client reference, and parent invocation where present. Verification should also confirm that audit review does not expose raw backend secrets or tokens and that stored evidence is sufficient for later reviewer inspection of why a request was allowed, clarified, denied, or stopped for approval.
items.title: Verify durable audit evidence and lineage across governed outcomes
items.rationale: Audit evidence and lineage are explicit baseline requirements, and a dedicated scenario calls out reviewable evidence, policy boundary visibility, and secret-safe posture. Exact retrieval interface details remain open, but evidence expectations are clear enough to verify.

items.body: Verification should demonstrate coverage for the full locked public capability inventory across the two public services, with scenario evidence spanning bounded reads, preview-only preparation, approval-required requests, release-note drafting, and denial behavior. Checks should confirm each canonical capability has at least one concrete verification path for happy-path behavior and one guard-path behavior such as clarification, denial, or approval stop where applicable. Verification should use the public governed surface as the primary contract anchor, with internal adapter checks treated only as supporting implementation evidence.
items.title: Verify scenario coverage across the canonical public capability inventory
items.rationale: The locked inventory is canonical and must be preserved exactly. The scenario set already covers the major behavior families, but the exact required depth per capability is not fully specified, so this block is slightly less certain.

watchouts: The locked baseline is explicit about governed outcomes, but the exact audit retrieval interface is still underspecified.
Developer evidence includes internal adapter operations, but the canonical public capability inventory must remain the verification anchor.
Some prepare and request flows reference backend mutation operations for preview generation; verification must prove no mutation occurs during preview or approval-stop paths.
Raw Jira REST, MCP, raw tool access, and raw JQL are non-goals and should be verified as explicit denials rather than omitted silently.
next_steps: Review and accept, edit, or reject each candidate verification block individually.
Decide whether internal adapter evidence is required in the same verification artifact or tracked separately as implementation evidence.
Confirm the minimum audit evidence fields that must be present across reads, clarifications, previews, approval stops, and denials.
questions_for_user: Should verification expectations cover only the public governed surface, or also include internal adapter seam checks as supporting evidence?
Do you want a separate verification block for audit evidence retrieval behavior, even though the exact audit review interface is not yet named?
Should denial verification require redirect guidance to a supported governed capability in every raw-access denial response, or only when a clear alternative exists?

capabilities.title: Search Backlog Context
capabilities.summary: Find bounded work context in an allowed Jira project without exposing raw JQL or unbounded issue export.

capabilities.title: Get Issue Context
capabilities.summary: Inspect one Jira issue with bounded actor-visible fields, comments, and history.

capabilities.title: Prepare Incident Bug
capabilities.summary: Prepare a Jira bug ticket from incident or defect context without creating it.

capabilities.title: Prepare Story
capabilities.summary: Prepare a Jira story preview with acceptance criteria before any issue is created.

capabilities.title: Prepare Subtask
capabilities.summary: Prepare a subtask under an existing parent issue without creating it.

capabilities.title: Prepare Customer Escalation Comment
capabilities.summary: Prepare a customer-safe Jira comment from incident or triage context without posting it.

capabilities.title: Request Workflow Transition
capabilities.summary: Request a governed Jira issue transition and stop for approval when policy requires it.

capabilities.title: Request Sprint Move
capabilities.summary: Request moving Jira issue(s) into a sprint or backlog with approval and actor-visible board scope.

capabilities.title: Request Assignee Change
capabilities.summary: Request reassignment of a Jira issue with backend user resolution, preview, approval, and audit.

capabilities.title: Request Issue Link
capabilities.summary: Request linking two Jira issues with a declared relationship and cross-project policy checks.

capabilities.title: Prepare Release Notes
capabilities.summary: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing or mutating Jira.

capabilities.title: Read Issue Context
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter read for bounded Jira issue fields, comments, and changelog context.

capabilities.title: Read Backlog Context
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter read for bounded Jira backlog search context without raw JQL exposure.

capabilities.title: Resolve Assignee
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter lookup that resolves an assignee reference to a Jira user identity.

capabilities.title: Execute Workflow Transition
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter mutation available only after governed approval for a Jira workflow transition.

capabilities.title: Execute Sprint Move
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter mutation available only after governed approval for moving Jira issues to a sprint.

capabilities.title: Execute Assignee Change
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter mutation available only after governed approval for changing a Jira issue assignee.

capabilities.title: Execute Issue Link
capabilities.summary: Internal adapter mutation available only after governed approval for linking Jira issues.

pm_artifacts.title: Runtime Policy Binding Proposal: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3

items.body: Runtime policy should allow only the locked public capability inventory on the public HTTP surface: bounded backlog search, single-issue context retrieval, preview-only issue/comment preparation, release-note preparation, and approval-gated change requests. The policy should explicitly deny raw Jira REST access, raw MCP operations, raw JQL exposure, unrestricted export behavior, and any agent-facing contract that exposes backend tool semantics directly.
items.title: Bind the public runtime surface to governed Jira capabilities only
items.rationale: The baseline and scenarios repeatedly require a governed fronting surface, safe denial of raw tool access, and no exposure of raw backend semantics.

items.body: Runtime policy should treat project keys, issue references, workflow statuses, sprints, assignees, releases, components, and link types as governed references resolved through backend catalogs where defined. If a required input is missing, ambiguous, or unresolved, the runtime outcome should be clarification_required or deny according to the reviewed input contract, and the system must not guess values.
items.title: Require clarification for missing, ambiguous, or unresolved business references
items.rationale: The locked requirements and developer evidence are explicit that governed Jira flows must clarify rather than guess, with per-input resolution behavior already drafted.

items.body: Runtime policy should bind jira.backlog.search_context, jira.issue.get_context, and jira.release_notes.prepare to bounded, actor-visible Jira data only. Search results should remain scoped to allowed project context and bounded limits; issue context should return bounded fields with comments by default and changelog only when requested or configured; release-note preparation should aggregate bounded issue context without publishing or mutating Jira.
items.title: Constrain read capabilities to actor-visible bounded context
items.rationale: The baseline defines bounded reads, actor-visible fields, no unbounded export, and non-mutating release-note preparation.

items.body: Runtime policy should mark jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, and jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare as prepare_only flows that may gather metadata or context behind the boundary but must stop at a draft or preview outcome. These bindings should prohibit direct issue creation or comment posting as part of the initial request and should preserve any one-time or session-bound delegation posture used for preview preparation.
items.title: Bind issue and comment preparation flows to preview-only outcomes
items.rationale: Both the baseline and developer evidence define these capabilities as preview-only, write-adjacent, and non-mutating at the public contract.

items.body: Runtime policy should mark jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, and jira.issue_link.request as approval-gated request flows. Each request should require a stated business reason, produce a preview of the intended change, apply policy checks before any backend mutation boundary, and return an explicit approval_stop outcome whenever approval is required.
items.title: Bind change request capabilities to explicit approval-stop behavior
items.rationale: The locked baseline and scenarios consistently describe these as governed request flows that stop for approval before execution.

items.body: Runtime policy should distinguish actor postures for triage, delivery coordination, release preparation, change approval, and audit review. Triage and delivery actors should be allowed bounded reads and relevant preview preparation; release preparation should be allowed release-note drafting; change approvers should be the posture eligible to review approval-gated requests; audit reviewers should have review access to governance evidence; non-review actors should be denied governance-control access not intended for them.
items.title: Apply role-aware runtime posture across reads, preparation, approval, and audit review
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires role-aware governance, but principal names and exact principal-to-role mapping are not yet locked.

items.body: Runtime policy should require durable and searchable audit evidence for governed Jira reads, previews, clarifications, denials, and approval stops. Evidence should capture capability identity, requested bounded scope or target, outcome type, applied policy boundary, and lineage fields including task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id. Secret posture should store references rather than tokens.
items.title: Require durable, searchable audit evidence on all governed outcomes
items.rationale: Audit durability, searchability, lineage, and secret-reference posture are all explicit in the locked baseline and supporting scenarios.

items.body: Runtime policy should preserve the public service split: Jira Fronting owns bounded reads, preview-only preparation, clarification stops, and release-note drafting; Jira Governance owns approval-gated change requests, raw-access denial enforcement at the governed surface, and governance-visible evidence for approvals and denials. Handoffs between the two services should preserve capability identity, governed outcome type, and backend boundary evidence.
items.title: Bind runtime enforcement to the two-service public ownership split
items.rationale: The locked service design defines a canonical two-service public shape with explicit ownership and handoff expectations.

pm_artifacts.title: Backend Binding Proposal: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3

items.body: For generated backend bindings, map public capabilities to the governed Jira HTTP surface described in integration evidence rather than exposing raw Jira REST semantics. Candidate endpoint bindings include backlog context search, issue context retrieval, issue/comment preview endpoints, workflow transition request, sprint move request, assignee change request, issue link request, and release notes preview. Preserve capability-first contracts at the public boundary while allowing internal translation to Jira REST or MCP behind the seam.
items.title: Bind public capabilities to governed Jira HTTP endpoints, not raw Jira APIs
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly denies raw Jira REST, MCP, and raw JQL exposure while the integration evidence provides governed endpoint-shaped operations for the same capabilities.

items.body: Bind Jira Fronting to bounded read and preview-only preparation capabilities: jira.backlog.search_context, jira.issue.get_context, jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare, and jira.release_notes.prepare. Bind Jira Governance to approval-gated request capabilities: jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, and jira.issue_link.request. Keep this routing explicit in backend bindings so generated code does not blur preview/read behavior with approval control behavior.
items.title: Route backend bindings by public service ownership
items.rationale: The locked service design is canonical for public capability ownership and coordination between Jira Fronting and Jira Governance.

items.body: Model downstream Jira access through an internal adapter seam that performs bounded reads, catalog-backed resolution, and post-approval execution calls. Candidate internal bindings include adapter reads for issue and backlog context, assignee resolution, and execution-only operations for workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link. Treat the adapter as hidden implementation plumbing, not part of the public capability inventory.
items.title: Use an internal Jira adapter seam for downstream Jira operations
items.rationale: Developer evidence names a jira-adapter with concrete read, resolve, and execute operations, while the public service shape intentionally excludes internal adapter seams.

items.body: Backend bindings for prepare and request capabilities should separate preview generation from mutation execution. For jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, and jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare, bindings should allow metadata lookup and draft shaping but stop before create_issue or add_comment execution. For jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, and jira.issue_link.request, bindings should produce a preview plus approval boundary and only permit execution through a distinct approved path.
items.title: Enforce non-mutating preview and approval-stop guardrails in bindings
items.rationale: The baseline repeatedly requires preview-only preparation and approval-gated changes, even though developer evidence references underlying mutating Jira operations.

items.body: Make catalog-backed resolution an explicit dependency in backend bindings for project keys, issue references, workflow statuses, sprints, assignees, releases, components, and link types. Resolution failures or ambiguity should return clarification or denial outcomes according to the capability contract rather than falling through to freeform Jira execution. This should apply consistently across reads, previews, and approval-gated requests.
items.title: Bind entity resolution to governed Jira catalogs before backend execution
items.rationale: The locked baseline requires governed backend resolution and explicitly forbids guessing unresolved business references.

items.body: Every backend binding should emit durable, searchable audit evidence capturing capability invoked, requested scope or target, outcome type, and backend boundary evidence, while propagating task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id. Bindings should use secret references only and avoid storing or exporting Jira tokens. Denials for raw access attempts and approval stops should be recorded with the same lineage model as successful bounded reads and previews.
items.title: Attach audit evidence, lineage, and secret-reference posture to every backend binding
items.rationale: Audit durability, searchability, lineage fields, signed trust mode, and secret-reference posture are all explicit in the locked baseline and integration evidence.

pm_artifacts.title: Verification Expectations Proposal: Jira Fronting Showcase 0.2.3

items.body: Verification should prove that backlog context search and single-issue context retrieval return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context through the governed surface. Checks should confirm that project, issue, status, and release references are resolved through governed catalogs or clarified when missing, ambiguous, or unresolved. Verification should also confirm that raw JQL is never exposed as the contract, unbounded export behavior is not available, comments are included where expected, and changelog/history is included only when requested or configured.
items.title: Verify bounded Jira read behavior and clarification handling
items.rationale: The baseline and scenarios are explicit about bounded reads, clarification instead of guessing, and denial of raw JQL or unrestricted export. Developer evidence provides concrete inputs and defaults for backlog search and issue context.

items.body: Verification should prove that incident bug, story, subtask, and customer escalation comment capabilities stop at draft or preview output and do not create issues or post comments during the initial request path. Checks should confirm required inputs are enforced, missing inputs trigger clarification, closed-value inputs deny unsupported values, and preview responses preserve governed capability identity rather than backend tool semantics. Where preview generation touches create metadata or issue context, verification should show that the runtime outcome remains write-adjacent only and records evidence that the flow stopped before mutation.
items.title: Verify preview-only issue and comment preparation flows
items.rationale: Preview-only preparation is a core product promise in the baseline and is reinforced by multiple safety scenarios. Developer evidence names the prepare-only posture, required inputs, and non-mutation business effects.

items.body: Verification should prove that workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link requests prepare a governed preview, require a stated business reason, and stop at the approval boundary before any Jira mutation occurs. Checks should confirm governed resolution of statuses, sprints, assignees, issue references, and link types; clarification on ambiguity; denial when unresolved values are out of policy; and preservation of actor-visible scope and policy checks such as cross-project link controls. Verification should also confirm that approval-stop is represented as an explicit outcome rather than an implicit failure or hidden backend hold.
items.title: Verify approval-gated Jira change request behavior
items.rationale: Approval-gated changes are central to the project summary and requirements. The scenario pack and developer evidence provide concrete request capabilities, required reason fields, and approval-required side-effect posture.

items.body: Verification should prove that requests for raw Jira REST, MCP operations, raw Jira tools, raw JQL exposure, unrestricted export, or similar out-of-policy access are denied intentionally and consistently. Checks should confirm the denial explains that only governed capabilities are available, does not leak backend operation names as the supported contract, and redirects the actor toward a governed capability when a valid alternative exists. Verification should treat denial as a first-class runtime outcome with audit evidence, not as a generic error path.
items.title: Verify explicit denial of raw Jira access and unsupported operations
items.rationale: Safe denial of raw tool access is explicit in the project summary, requirements, service responsibilities, and denial scenario. This is a key non-goal guard for the governed fronting design.

items.body: Verification should prove that governed Jira interactions produce durable, searchable evidence for reads, clarifications, previews, approval stops, and denials. At minimum, checks should confirm evidence captures the invoked capability, requested bounded scope or target, outcome type, applied policy boundary, and lineage identifiers such as task, invocation, client reference, and parent invocation where present. Verification should also confirm that audit review does not expose raw backend secrets or tokens and that stored evidence is sufficient for later reviewer inspection of why a request was allowed, clarified, denied, or stopped for approval.
items.title: Verify durable audit evidence and lineage across governed outcomes
items.rationale: Audit evidence and lineage are explicit baseline requirements, and a dedicated scenario calls out reviewable evidence, policy boundary visibility, and secret-safe posture. Exact retrieval interface details remain open, but evidence expectations are clear enough to verify.

items.body: Verification should demonstrate coverage for the full locked public capability inventory across the two public services, with scenario evidence spanning bounded reads, preview-only preparation, approval-required requests, release-note drafting, and denial behavior. Checks should confirm each canonical capability has at least one concrete verification path for happy-path behavior and one guard-path behavior such as clarification, denial, or approval stop where applicable. Verification should use the public governed surface as the primary contract anchor, with internal adapter checks treated only as supporting implementation evidence.
items.title: Verify scenario coverage across the canonical public capability inventory
items.rationale: The locked inventory is canonical and must be preserved exactly. The scenario set already covers the major behavior families, but the exact required depth per capability is not fully specified, so this block is slightly less certain.

Verification should also confirm that raw JQL is never exposed as the contract, unbounded export behavior is not available, comments are included where expected, and changelog/history is included only when requested or configured.

coverage.label: Governed Behavior Summary
coverage.detail: The product exposes governed Jira capabilities instead of raw Jira operations. Reads return bounded actor-visible context, write-adjacent actions produce previews or requests rather than direct execution, missing required inputs trigger clarification, and unsafe raw access requests are explicitly denied.
coverage.mapping_note: This PM governed behavior summary is formalized explicitly in Product Intent Formalization on the Service Formalization page.
coverage.mapping_target_label: Developer Design > Service Formalization > Product Intent Formalization > Governed Behavior Summary

coverage.label: Approval Posture Summary

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not expose raw Jira REST, MCP, or raw tool access to agents or end users.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The brief repeatedly states that the agent must see governed Jira capabilities rather than native operations, and that raw tool access should be safely denied.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not support raw JQL entry or unbounded Jira issue export.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The backlog and issue-context capabilities are defined as bounded, actor-visible reads without raw JQL exposure or unbounded export.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not create, transition, assign, link, comment on, or move Jira issues automatically without the intended preview or approval stop.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Write-adjacent actions are described as prepare-only or approval-required, with explicit stops before execution.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not guess missing required inputs for Jira actions; require clarification instead.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The governance posture explicitly says missing required inputs should trigger clarification rather than guessing.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not publish or distribute release notes automatically; only prepare bounded drafts.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Release-note capability scope is limited to preparation from bounded Jira issues and explicitly excludes publishing or mutation.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not return Jira data beyond bounded actor-visible context.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Reads are defined as bounded and actor-visible, and the project centers on governed Jira context rather than broad data retrieval.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not treat audit evidence as optional or secondary to convenience automation.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The project summary explicitly includes audit evidence as part of the canonical governed showcase.

business_goal_bindings.evidence_signal: Scenario and runtime evidence demonstrate: Enable bounded, actor-visible Jira context retrieval for backlog and issue review.
business_goal_bindings.verification_strategy: Verify that the selected services preserve the business goal: Enable bounded, actor-visible Jira context retrieval for backlog and issue review.

business_goal_bindings.business_goal: Let users prepare Jira issue creation, comments, release notes, and change requests as previews before execution.

business_goal_bindings.verification_strategy: Verify that the selected services preserve the business goal: Let users prepare Jira issue creation, comments, release notes, and change requests as previews before execution.

business_goal_bindings.verification_strategy: Verify that the selected services preserve the business goal: Require explicit approval for governed Jira changes such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Users can retrieve bounded Jira backlog or issue context through governed capabilities without being exposed to raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL-style access.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows backlog search and issue context flows return actor-visible bounded results, while requests for raw Jira or raw query/tool access are explicitly denied or redirected to governed capabilities.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Issue, subtask, bug, story, customer comment, workflow, sprint, assignee, and issue-link flows produce previews or approval requests before any Jira mutation is allowed.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative flow review shows write-adjacent actions stop at preview or approval request states and do not execute direct Jira changes without the required approval boundary.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: When required inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved, the product asks for clarification instead of guessing or silently failing.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Review scenarios across search, issue lookup, assignee resolution, and change-request flows show explicit clarification prompts for missing or ambiguous project, issue, user, sprint, status, or relationship inputs.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Approval-required Jira change requests provide enough preview detail and business reason context for a change approver to review the intended action before execution.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review of workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link request examples shows the target change, affected Jira items, and stated reason are visible at approval time.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The showcase produces reviewable audit evidence for governed reads, denials, previews, approvals, and approved change execution paths.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Audit review can inspect traceable records for representative read, denial, preview, approval-stop, and approved-action scenarios without reconstructing the flow from raw backend logs alone.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Release-note preparation produces a bounded draft from Jira issue and release context without publishing externally or mutating Jira.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows release-note preparation returns a draft artifact based on bounded Jira inputs and does not trigger Jira updates or external dispatch.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-only context retrieval can proceed within bounded scope, while write-adjacent actions either stop at preview or require explicit approval before any Jira mutation is executed. Higher-impact changes are approval-gated and auditable.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product exposes governed Jira capabilities instead of raw Jira operations. Reads return bounded actor-visible context, write-adjacent actions produce previews or requests rather than direct execution, missing required inputs trigger clarification, and unsafe raw access requests are explicitly denied.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search backlog context, inspect issue context, prepare incident bugs, prepare subtasks, and prepare customer escalation comments within governed input rules and clarification flows.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Operational user who inspects bounded Jira issue and backlog context and prepares issue or comment drafts without direct raw Jira tool access.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can initiate preview-oriented and approval-gated requests but should stop at explicit approval boundaries for governed mutations.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see bounded Jira project, issue, comment, changelog, and backlog context exposed through governed capabilities, plus previews and audit-relevant request context. Should not see raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL/tool access.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue links through governed preview and request flows with stated business reasons.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Planning-oriented user who prepares and requests governed changes to issue workflow, sprint placement, assignee, and issue relationships.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can assemble and submit approval-required change requests, but execution authority is separate unless you confirm this actor is also an approver.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see actor-visible workflow, sprint, assignee-resolution, and issue-link preview context needed to understand proposed changes, but not raw backend operations or unrestricted Jira administration surfaces.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can prepare release notes drafts from governed Jira release and issue queries and tailor drafts for internal, customer, or executive audiences where allowed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Release-focused user who prepares bounded release notes from Jira issue context without publishing or mutating Jira.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can prepare release-note drafts directly; approval or publication authority is not specified in the brief and should be clarified if needed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see bounded release and issue context sufficient to draft release notes for an intended audience, without raw export or unrestricted issue extraction.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests and decide whether they should proceed under governance.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Reviewing actor who evaluates governed Jira change requests before any approval-required mutation proceeds.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the explicit approval boundary for approval-required Jira changes, but exact authority by action type is not specified in the source and should be confirmed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the proposed change, target issue or sprint context, stated reason, and supporting audit evidence needed to approve or deny governed Jira mutations.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review evidence of bounded reads, prepared write-adjacent actions, approval stops, and explicit denials of raw Jira/JQL/tool access.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Oversight actor who reviews audit evidence for governed Jira access, prepared changes, approvals, and denials.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Typically not the operational approver unless your process combines audit and approval responsibilities; this should be clarified.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect audit evidence showing what governed capability was used, what was requested or denied, and where approval boundaries were enforced. Should not gain raw Jira tool access through the audit role.

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded Jira backlog and issue context needed for triage without exposing raw JQL, raw tool access, or unbounded export.
permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_triage_operator bounded access to Bounded Jira Context should return bounded_result. Allow bounded Jira backlog and issue context needed for triage without exposing raw JQL, raw tool access, or unbounded export. Matches bounded context search and issue context inspection in the brief.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow preparation previews for incident bugs, subtasks, and customer escalation comments without creating issues or posting comments directly.
permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_triage_operator bounded access to Issue and Comment Preparation should return bounded_result. Allow preparation previews for incident bugs, subtasks, and customer escalation comments without creating issues or posting comments directly. Preparation remains preview-only and non-mutating from the actor perspective.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests to be prepared with preview and audit context, then stop for explicit approval before execution.
permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_delivery_coordinator approval_required access to Governed Change Requests should return approval_stop. Allow workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue-link requests to be prepared with preview and audit context, then stop for explicit approval before execution. Covers approval-required write-adjacent change requests.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of release-note drafts from bounded Jira issue and release context without publishing or mutating Jira.
permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_release_preparer bounded access to Release Note Preparation should return bounded_result. Allow preparation of release-note drafts from bounded Jira issue and release context without publishing or mutating Jira. Read-only draft generation only.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Receive governed change-request packages with preview and rationale, and act as the explicit approval boundary before any Jira mutation proceeds.
permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_change_approver approval_required access to Governed Change Requests should return approval_stop. Receive governed change-request packages with preview and rationale, and act as the explicit approval boundary before any Jira mutation proceeds. Frames the approver as the approval checkpoint, not a raw tool operator.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow access to governed audit evidence and review artifacts for Jira fronting decisions, approvals, and denials.
permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_audit_reviewer allowed access to Governance and Audit Controls should return direct_result. Allow access to governed audit evidence and review artifacts for Jira fronting decisions, approvals, and denials. Audit review is evidence-focused rather than operational control.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Deny requests for raw Jira REST access, MCP access, raw JQL execution, or direct tool use outside the governed capability surface.
permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_triage_operator denied access to Governance and Audit Controls should return deny_request. Deny requests for raw Jira REST access, MCP access, raw JQL execution, or direct tool use outside the governed capability surface. Applies the fronting boundary to business-facing actors.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Deny direct raw Jira, MCP, and raw JQL access even when coordinating change requests; use governed request surfaces only.
permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_delivery_coordinator denied access to Governance and Audit Controls should return deny_request. Deny direct raw Jira, MCP, and raw JQL access even when coordinating change requests; use governed request surfaces only. Prevents coordinators from bypassing approval-gated flows.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Deny raw Jira/JQL/tool access for release-note work; release-note preparation must use bounded governed capabilities only.

Deny raw Jira/JQL/tool access for release-note work; release-note preparation must use bounded governed capabilities only. Keeps release-note drafting within the fronted read-only surface.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Users can retrieve bounded Jira backlog or issue context through governed capabilities without being exposed to raw Jira REST, MCP operations, or unbounded JQL-style access.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows backlog search and issue context flows return actor-visible bounded results, while requests for raw Jira or raw query/tool access are explicitly denied or redirected to governed capabilities.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Issue, subtask, bug, story, customer comment, workflow, sprint, assignee, and issue-link flows produce previews or approval requests before any Jira mutation is allowed.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative flow review shows write-adjacent actions stop at preview or approval request states and do not execute direct Jira changes without the required approval boundary.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: When required inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved, the product asks for clarification instead of guessing or silently failing.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Review scenarios across search, issue lookup, assignee resolution, and change-request flows show explicit clarification prompts for missing or ambiguous project, issue, user, sprint, status, or relationship inputs.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Approval-required Jira change requests provide enough preview detail and business reason context for a change approver to review the intended action before execution.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review of workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link request examples shows the target change, affected Jira items, and stated reason are visible at approval time.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The showcase produces reviewable audit evidence for governed reads, denials, previews, approvals, and approved change execution paths.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Audit review can inspect traceable records for representative read, denial, preview, approval-stop, and approved-action scenarios without reconstructing the flow from raw backend logs alone.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Release-note preparation produces a bounded draft from Jira issue and release context without publishing externally or mutating Jira.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows release-note preparation returns a draft artifact based on bounded Jira inputs and does not trigger Jira updates or external dispatch.
